# ELEVATED PROGRAM v2 — OICC (auto-generated by deep 14-agent workflow + verified web research + RUNNABLE code)

> Round 2. Deeper than v1: verified the scooping papers by fetching them, ran a novelty tournament, integrated a THIRD project (India NCRB), produced real proofs + a runnable proof-of-concept (see experiments/), and survived a red-team by a reviewer expert in BOTH measurement-error identification and conformal inference. Read PART V (final verdict) first.

---

# PART I — CHOSEN CORE (OICC)

# THE DEFINITIVE CORE: OICC — Over-Identification-Calibrated Conformal Deconvolution

## Director's selection

Of the three cores on the table, only one contains a **theorem** rather than a **pipeline**, and only a theorem clears the prior ceiling. I select **Core Proposal 2's OICC**, but *not* in its naive "inherit the over-ID slack as a sensitivity ball" form — that form is provably blind in the one direction that matters and would sink us a third time. I adopt OICC's **matched pair**: a constructive upper bound on the *identifiable* dependence directions + an imported impossibility lower bound proving the residual is *irreducibly* unidentifiable. Core Proposal 1 supplies the identification ladder and the conformal-gap machinery that OICC's theorem instantiates; Core Proposal 3 supplies the data-reality constraints (India state-year resolution ceiling, mechanism-independent channel selection) and the cross-national mechanism-invariance test that is our strongest external-validity asset. That composition is the spine.

The one-sentence thesis the whole paper defends: **we do not make conditional independence testable; we prove that the multi-channel data exhausts every identifiable direction of the assumption, quarantine the unfalsifiable remainder into a single provably-minimal user knob, and deliver conformal coverage of the deconvolved latent that degrades visibly in exactly that knob.**

---

## 1. The core object

Let $\lambda(a,t)$ be the latent true victimization rate in area $a$, period $t$. On the log scale $\theta_{at} = \log\lambda(a,t)$. We observe $K \ge 3$ **mechanism-independent** channels
$$
Y^c_{at} = \alpha_c + \beta_c\,\theta_{at} + \varepsilon^c_{at}, \qquad c=1,\dots,K,
$$
a one-factor measurement model. **OICC** is the estimator that (i) deconvolves $f_\theta$ and the channel error laws from the joint characteristic function, (ii) decomposes the admissible space of conditional-independence violations into a **detectable** subspace (data-driven budget) and a **factor-aligned common-mode** subspace (irreducible user knob), and (iii) returns a per-area conformal set for $\theta_{at}$ whose width is $(\hat\delta_\perp,\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}})$-calibrated, with $\hat\delta_\perp$ estimated from the over-identifying restrictions and $\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$ the sole surviving assumption — proven minimal.

---

## 2. Assumptions

- **(A1) Conditional independence given the latent.** $\varepsilon^1,\dots,\varepsilon^K$ mutually independent given $(\theta, X)$. *This is the assumption that was untestable last round with one anchor.* OICC does not assume it away — it splits it.
- **(A2) Completeness / non-vanishing CF.** Kotlarski regularity (CFs of $\theta$ and each $\varepsilon^c$ non-vanishing); in the nonclassical case, Hu–Schennach bounded-completeness of the integral operator.
- **(A3) Normalization.** Scale $\beta_1=1$; location $\mathbb{E}[\varepsilon^c]=0$ (classical) or $\mathrm{median}(\varepsilon^c\mid\theta)=0$ (Hu–Schennach nonclassical). Police under-recording is nonclassical — error correlates with the true rate — so **Hu–Schennach, not classical Kotlarski, is the backbone**.
- **(A4) Exchangeability** of calibration and test units for the conformal step (separate from A1–A3; handled with Kluger-style spatial/clustered treatment).
- **(A5) Bounded common-mode budget.** The factor-aligned component of the error covariance satisfies $\|\Delta_\parallel\|\le \Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$. This is the *only* irreducible user assumption; the theorem's lower bound proves no data can shrink it.

---

## 3. Identification (the ladder)

**Two channels, classical error → point-identify $f_\theta$ (Kotlarski / Li–Vuong 1998).** From $\varphi_{Y^1,Y^2}(s,t)=\varphi_\theta(s+t)\varphi_{\varepsilon^1}(s)\varphi_{\varepsilon^2}(t)$ and $\varphi_{\varepsilon^2}'(0)=0$,
$$
\varphi_\theta(t)=\exp\!\Big(\!\int_0^t \tfrac{\partial_2\varphi_{Y^1,Y^2}(s,0)}{\varphi_{Y^1,Y^2}(s,0)}\,ds\Big).
$$

**Two channels + nonclassical error → Hu–Schennach 2008**, via eigen-decomposition of the operator linking observed conditional densities under (A2) and mode/median-zero normalization. This is the operative regime for crime.

**Three+ channels → loadings free + surplus (Bonhomme–Robin 2010; Cunha–Heckman–Schennach 2010).** Higher-order cumulants identify $\beta_c$ and the factor law. **Load-bearing correction that must appear in the paper:** with *exactly* 3 indicators the one-factor model is **just-identified in second moments** ($\mathrm{Cov}(Y^j,Y^k)=\beta_j\beta_k\sigma_\theta^2$, 3 eqns / 3 unknowns, 0 df). The over-identifying content with $K=3$ lives **entirely in the higher-order cumulants / full joint CF**, not the covariance matrix. Covariance tetrad tests need $K\ge4$. State this or a factor-analysis referee sinks the test.

**Resolution ceiling (buildability constraint, non-negotiable):** the channels must measure the *same* latent unit. In India every mechanism-independent channel (complaints-against-police, custodial-death, HR-violation, distress-calls PC3) is **state-year**; the district IPC series can only enter as a downscaling covariate. So the India estimand is a **state-year rate field (~34–36 areas × 14 yrs ≈ 480 cells)**, and the US estimand is metro-month. Declared up front.

---

## 4. The testability result — decomposition + matched impossibility (the crux)

This is where we beat the prior fatal objection, and where we must be surgically precise.

**Setup.** Admissible dependence perturbation $\Delta=\mathrm{Cov}(U)_{\text{off-diag}}$ splits orthogonally,
$$
\Delta \;=\; \underbrace{\Delta_\perp}_{\text{detectable}} \;\oplus\; \underbrace{\Delta_\parallel}_{\text{factor-aligned, rank-one} \,\propto\, \beta\beta^\top}.
$$

**(Upper / constructive — data exhausts the detectable subspace).** The pairwise Kotlarski recoveries $\{\hat f_\theta^{(jk)}\}$ must coincide under the maintained model; their discrepancy is a Hansen–Sargan $J$-statistic (equivalently a CF/cumulant over-ID restriction à la Kato–Sasaki–Ura 2021) that is a **consistent estimator of a monotone functional of $\|\Delta_\perp\|$**. This yields a one-sided $(1-\beta)$ bound $\hat\delta_\perp$. Reported **per area as a first-class tripwire**: it fires precisely in high-disparity cells.

**(Lower / matching impossibility — imported from the Kill-1 lemma).** For any two admissible laws differing *only* in $\Delta_\parallel$, the observed joint law of $(Y^1,\dots,Y^K)$ is **identical for every $K$** — factor-aligned common-mode dependence is absorbed into the estimated latent factor itself. Therefore **no estimator and no specification test, at any number of channels, can bound $\|\Delta_\parallel\|$ from data.** $\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$ is irreducibly a user knob.

**What this buys, stated exactly.** The over-ID test is a **falsification lever the prior program did not have**: rejection falsifies the conjunction $\{\text{one-factor}\wedge\text{A1}\wedge\text{A3}\}$ in identifiable directions. But it falsifies a *conjunction*, not A1 alone; non-rejection never verifies A1 (finite power, Shah–Peters 2020 hardness); and it has **provably zero power against $\Delta_\parallel$** — the exact high-disparity failure mode. The contribution is not "CI is testable." It is: **the data-driven part exhausts everything identifiable, and we prove the remainder is unidentifiable for anyone.** That is the *provably-minimal residual assumption* — a quarantine with a proof of tightness, not a dissolution.

---

## 5. The conformal wrapper (the theorem)

**The pitfall that separates OICC from PPI / Csillag / Jin–Ren–Candès:** those methods observe the target $Y$ on a gold-standard/calibration sample. Here $\theta_{at}$ is **never observed on any sample** — so no nonconformity score $|\theta-\hat\theta|$ exists, and conformalizing against any channel $Y^c$ yields valid coverage for the **biased proxy**, not $\theta$. That is the trap. Finite-sample distribution-free coverage of $\theta$ is **impossible without a label** — and we state that impossibility as a theorem, not hide it.

**What is honestly provable — coverage up to an estimable gap plus a budgeted dependence term:**

> **Theorem (OICC coverage).** Take channel 1 as observable pivot, $Y^1=\theta+\varepsilon^1$, error law $f_{\varepsilon^1}$ identified by §3. Let $\widehat C_\alpha$ be the split-conformal set for the observable $Y^1_{a,T+1}$ (valid by A4). Deconvolve $\hat f_{\varepsilon^1}$ to map $\widehat C_\alpha \to \widehat C^{\theta}_\alpha$, then inflate by the identified-set diameter $D(\hat\delta_\perp,\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}})$ over the ball $\{\|\Delta_\perp\|\le\hat\delta_\perp,\ \|\Delta_\parallel\|\le\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}\}$. Then for any true perturbation in the ball,
> $$
> \mathbb{P}\big(\theta_{a,T+1}\in \widehat C^{\theta,\Gamma}_\alpha\big)\;\ge\;1-\alpha-\underbrace{\mathrm{TV}(\hat f_{\varepsilon^1},f_{\varepsilon^1})}_{\text{deconvolution error, }o_P(1)}-\underbrace{\beta}_{\text{over-ID bound level}}-o_P(1).
> $$

The entire cost of the missing label is localized in $\mathrm{TV}(\hat f_{\varepsilon^1},f_{\varepsilon^1})$, **consistently estimable with known rates** (Kato–Sasaki–Ura robust deconvolution). The residual dependence enters *only* through $D(\hat\delta_\perp,\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}})$: where the over-ID tripwire fires, $\hat\delta_\perp$ grows and the set widens — **coverage degrades visibly, not silently.** $\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$ is the sole knob, and §4's lower bound certifies it cannot be shrunk.

**This single object — finite-sample conformal coverage of a deconvolved latent target under a bounded conditional-dependence budget, with the budget's identifiable component estimated-and-tested from the over-identifying channels and its unidentifiable component proven minimal — is the theorem the prior verifications named as "the one path to break the ceiling," and it does not exist in the literature.**

---

## 6. Novelty delta vs every named scooping paper

| Scooping paper | What it owns | OICC's exact delta |
|---|---|---|
| **Jin–Ren–Candès (PNAS 2023)** conformal sensitivity | $\Gamma$ entirely user-specified; untestable-by-design knob on unmeasured confounding of a **counterfactual outcome** | OICC **data-drives** the detectable component $\hat\delta_\perp$ from over-ID restrictions and **shrinks the user knob to the provably-minimal factor-aligned subspace**; target is a **latent construct**, not a counterfactual; no measurement model in JRC |
| **PPI / PPI++ (Science 2023)** | corrects ML-imputed estimand using **gold-standard observed $Y$** | OICC's target is **never observed on any sample**; no rectifier possible; cost localized in an estimable deconvolution TV-gap instead |
| **Csillag et al. (2025)** conformal-powered PPI | conformal imputation of a **missing-but-observable label** $Y$; validity conditional on untested exchangeability | OICC identifies a **latent-rate distribution with no label** and **tests** its key assumption in identifiable directions via over-ID |
| **Cheng et al. (ICML 2024)** feedback identification | correct feedback ID via **logged predictions** | OICC needs no logging; and where feedback is unlogged, §4's lower bound proves the latent is non-identifiable (imported Kill-1 lemma) |
| **Kluger et al. (2025)** spatial/clustered PPI | spatial correction with observed labels | used as a **component** for the A4 spatial-exchangeability accounting; does not target a latent/deconvolved field |
| **Chugg et al. (NeurIPS 2023)** auditing fairness by betting | anytime-valid fairness e-processes | OICC's sequential over-ID monitor is a **demoted corollary** (universal inference ∘ over-ID moments); inherits Shah–Peters powerlessness vs common-mode, so **not** claimed as contribution |
| **Kotlarski 1967 / Li–Vuong 1998 / Schennach 2004 / Hu–Schennach 2008 / Cunha–Heckman–Schennach 2010** | nonparametric deconvolution & nonlinear factor identification | **borrowed wholesale**; OICC claims **zero** deconvolution novelty — only the composition atop it |
| **MSE / LCMCR (Manrique-Vallier 2016; HRDAG/Ball)**; identifiability (Jones–Johndrow–Manrique-Vallier 2020) | latent **count** of a closed population via individual-level **list overlap** | OICC needs **no record linkage** (channels are area-level counts, unlinkable): count→**rate-field**, overlap→**cross-channel covariation**, size-estimation→**conformal forecasting**. Cited as nearest prior art, explicitly *not* branded MSE |
| **Proximal causal / negative controls (Miao et al.; Tchetgen Tchetgen et al.; DANCE)** | extra proxies **point-identify** under completeness (de-biasing) | OICC delivers a **partial-ID robustness ball + conformal wrap + detectable/undetectable decomposition + minimality proof**, none of which proximal inference provides |

**Net novelty claim, defensible verbatim:** *the first sensitivity analysis whose robustness radius is data-driven on the over-identifiable subspace and provably-minimal user-specified on the unidentifiable factor-aligned complement, wrapping a deconvolved latent target in conformal coverage with an estimable gap — validated cross-nationally under structurally different bias mechanisms.*

---

## 7. Buildability on the real data

- **US spine (Projects A/B):** police records + **NCVS victimization survey** (respondent-recall filter, mechanism-independent anchor) + **911 calls-for-service** (public-initiation filter). Real Chicago 1.33M / NYC 1.51M records confirmed by prior red-team; conformal stack + Gamma-sensitivity + tripwire already exist in-repo and are reused. Honest engineering obstacle to flag: **NCVS is not published at fine sub-national geography** — the area-level survey channel requires small-area estimation, a real cost, not a given.
- **India spine (Project C), verified on disk:** `crime/01_District_wise_crimes_committed_IPC_2001_2012.csv` (+2013/+2014, district police records); `crime/27_Nature_of_complaints_received_by_police.csv` field **`PC3_Distress_call_over_phone_No_100`** (India 911-equivalent, state-year); `25_Complaints_against_police.csv`, `35_Human_rights_violation_by_police.csv`, `40_01..05_Custodial_death_*.csv` (independent state-violence channels). The custodial/HR channel's error is **anti-correlated** with police-records error (worst-recording states are highest-violence) — the strongest available independence structure and the substitute for India's missing survey.
- **Channel-selection rule (substantive, not rhetorical):** select by *generating mechanism*, not data source. Police records / arrests / cases collapse to one institutional filter and count as **one** channel. The independent triad must cross institutions — this is why the US triad is the identification workhorse and India contributes the state-level institutional-bias channel plus the cross-national axis.
- **Cross-national mechanism-invariance test (the impact multiplier):** US under-recording is **victim-side** (non-reporting), India's is **police-side** (burking / FIR refusal). A single method whose over-ID tripwire passes in low-disparity and fires in high-disparity cells in *both* countries, and whose record-vs-latent gap tracks each country's *different, known* bias direction, is identifying something mechanism-invariant — a robustness test that **cannot be gamed by a country-specific confound because the confounds differ.** Pre-register: deconvolved gap must correlate with independent disparity proxies (US immigrant share / mistrust; India state custodial-death rate); if not, the method fails publicly.

---

## 8. Residual honest limitation (the one paragraph that must survive any referee)

OICC **does not dissolve** the untestable-anchor objection — it **quarantines and bounds** it. Factor-aligned common-mode dependence — systematic under-recording that co-loads on the latent in the same direction across channels — is **provably unfalsifiable** (absorbed into the estimated factor; §4 lower bound). This is the prior fatal objection surviving in new clothing, and it is worst in exactly the sparse, high-disparity cells where the over-ID test is least powered (the continuous analogue of the LCMCR non-identifiability degeneracy, arXiv 2008.09865, which we cite and differentiate). What OICC delivers is strictly this and nothing more: the killer assumption goes from **untestable in principle** to **falsifiable-in-identifiable-directions with a proven-minimal, user-specified residual $\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$**, and coverage degrades **visibly** in that residual rather than silently. We never write "makes conditional independence testable"; we write "provably minimal residual assumption."

**Honest ratings.** Constructive half only → novelty ~7.0, publication ~8.0 (KDD-ADS / FAccT / AOAS / J. Quant. Criminology) — reads as "conformal + estimated sensitivity radius." **Matched pair (constructive upper + impossibility lower bound proving minimality)** → novelty ~7.5–8.0, publication ~8.0–8.5, **credible NeurIPS-main / D&B borderline**, because the contribution is a *tight two-sided theorem* (the first provably-minimal sensitivity analysis), not a pipeline. The build lives or dies on two non-negotiable proofs: (i) the $\Delta_\perp\oplus\Delta_\parallel$ decomposition with "over-ID power $\propto\|\Delta_\perp\|$, zero on $\Delta_\parallel$" in the Hu–Schennach nonclassical regime (state it as a local/first-order result if that is all that holds — the primitive still stands), and (ii) the deconvolution-error accounting ($\mathrm{TV}$ term + spatial exchangeability) into the coverage budget. Deliver both and the ceiling breaks by a defensible +1 to +1.5; deliver only the pipeline and the honest ceiling is KDD-ADS/FAccT/AOAS.

**Relevant paths:** `C:/Users/kamle/OneDrive/Desktop/crime-detection-ai/data/crime/01_District_wise_crimes_committed_IPC_2001_2012.csv`; `.../data/crime/27_Nature_of_complaints_received_by_police.csv` (PC3 distress-call channel); `.../data/25_Complaints_against_police.csv`, `.../data/35_Human_rights_violation_by_police.csv`, `.../data/40_01_Custodial_death_person_remanded.csv`; US pipelines `C:/Users/kamle/OneDrive/Desktop/PCC project/` and `C:/Users/kamle/OneDrive/Desktop/PCC best/ctc_hstgt_v7_godtier/`.

Environments balance (52 `\begin` / 52 `\end`), inline-math delimiters are even (790). No LaTeX toolchain is installed on this machine, so I couldn't produce a PDF, but the structural checks pass.

## Delivered

`C:/Users/kamle/OneDrive/Desktop/PCC project/theory/OICC_identification_theorems.tex` — a self-contained, compile-ready Part I theorem document. I verified the three independent India channels first: the exact NCRB field is `PC3_Distress_call_over_phoneNo_100_etc` (note: the brief's `PC3_Distress_call_over_phone_No_100` is slightly off — I used the on-disk name), plus `25_Complaints_against_police`, `35_Human_rights_violation_by_police`, `40_01_Custodial_death_person_remanded` all confirmed at state-year resolution.

## What the document proves (and refuses to over-claim)

- **§3 Theorem 1 (Kotlarski, classical):** full CF proof of point-identification of $f_\theta$ from $K\ge2$ channels, with the explicit estimator equation. Includes Remark that two channels **cannot** identify unknown loadings.
- **§4 Theorem 2 (Hu–Schennach, nonclassical):** the operative regime — proof-sketch of the operator-diagonalization identity `L_{W,y|Z}(L_{W|Z})^{-1} = L_{W|θ} D_{y|θ} L_{W|θ}^{-1}`, since police under-recording is error-correlates-with-truth.
- **§5 the load-bearing correction you flagged:** at exactly $K=3$ the one-factor model is **just-identified in second moments (0 df)** — tetrad tests need $K\ge4$; over-ID content lives in higher cumulants / full joint CF. Stated explicitly so a factor-analysis referee can't sink it.
- **§5 Theorem 3 + Prop 1 — two codeable specification tests:** the functional CF statistic $T_n^{\mathrm{CF}}$ (null = weighted sum of $\chi^2_1$, delta-method proof) and the GMM/Hansen–Sargan $T_n^{\mathrm J}$ (null $\chi^2_{q-p}$), both with exact estimator equations and bootstrap critical values.
- **§6 the matched impossibility (the crux):** Lemma (common-mode absorbed into the factor → **unidentifiable at every $K$**, all-orders proof) + Theorem (over-ID power is $\propto\|\Delta_\perp\|$, **exactly zero** against $\Delta_\parallel$). This is the two-sided result that beats the prior ceiling.
- **§7 the untestable residual** (5 items U1–U5) and **§8 hostile-referee section:** the exact answer to "how many channels to test a hidden confounder?" — **$K=4$** for non-proportional, **$\infty$ is not enough** for factor-aligned (needs an external exclusion restriction, which we decline to assume); plus zero-inflation (ZINB-as-nonclassical-kernel) and spatial-dependence (identification unaffected, inference needs spatial-HAC/block-bootstrap).

## Honest caveats on the artifact

- It is **Part I only** (identification + spec test), as the task scoped. The conformal coverage theorem is referenced as a Part II companion, not included.
- Proofs of Theorems 2–3 are rigorous **sketches** (delta-method / Hadamard-differentiability / KL expansion named, not fully expanded); Theorems 1 and the impossibility Lemma are complete. A journal submission would need the empirical-CF-process regularity and the ZINB-completeness conditions written out in full — these are flagged in-text as maintained.
- I could not run `pdflatex` here. Environment/math-delimiter balance passes, but you should compile once locally to catch any residual issue before circulating.

One correction worth your attention beyond the filename: the brief's claim that the over-ID test "makes conditional independence testable" is deliberately **not** asserted anywhere in the document — per §6/§8 it can only falsify the *conjunction* {one-factor ∧ A1 ∧ A3} in identifiable directions, which is the defensible claim.

===== PROOF DOC =====

I'll write this as a self-contained, LaTeX-ready section. This is a theory-authoring task, so I'm producing the mathematics directly rather than exploring the repos. I've checked each derivation for circularity and sign-correctness; where a claim only holds locally or up to an estimable gap, it is stated as such.

---

```latex
\section{The OICC Conformal Layer: Coverage of a Deconvolved Latent Target with an Over-Identification-Calibrated Sensitivity Radius}
\label{sec:oicc-conformal}

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Preamble assumed in the master file:
% \usepackage{amsmath,amssymb,amsthm,bm}
% \newtheorem{theorem}{Theorem}[section]
% \newtheorem{proposition}[theorem]{Proposition}
% \newtheorem{lemma}[theorem]{Lemma}
% \newtheorem{corollary}[theorem]{Corollary}
% \theoremstyle{definition}\newtheorem{definition}[theorem]{Definition}
% \theoremstyle{remark}\newtheorem{remark}[theorem]{Remark}
% \newtheorem{assumption}{Assumption}[section]
% ------------------------------------------------------------------

\subsection{Setup and the missing-label obstruction}

We observe $K\ge 3$ mechanism-independent channels of a common latent log-rate
$\theta_i\in\mathbb{R}$ over units $i$ indexing (area $a$, period $t$):
\begin{equation}
Y^c_i \;=\; \alpha_c + \beta_c\,\theta_i + \varepsilon^c_i,
\qquad c=1,\dots,K,
\label{eq:factor}
\end{equation}
with the standard one-factor normalization $\alpha_1=0,\ \beta_1=1$, so that
\begin{equation}
Y^1_i \;=\; \theta_i + \varepsilon^1_i .
\label{eq:pivot}
\end{equation}
Channel $1$ is the \emph{observable pivot} (in the US spine, the NCVS
victimization channel; in the India spine, the state--year distress-call
channel \texttt{PC3}). The latent $\theta_i$ is \emph{never observed on any
unit}. Consequently the ideal split-conformal nonconformity score
$|\theta_i-\hat\theta_i|$ is not computable on any calibration unit, and any
score built directly on a channel $Y^c$ conformalizes the \emph{biased proxy},
not $\theta$. This is the obstruction we must route around; we first record that
it cannot be routed around \emph{for free}.

\begin{proposition}[No free label; impossibility of finite-sample distribution-free latent coverage]
\label{prop:impossible}
Fix $\alpha\in(0,1)$ and let $\mathcal{Q}$ be the class of joint laws of
$(\theta,Y^1,\dots,Y^K)$ consistent with \eqref{eq:factor}. There is no
measurable set map $\widehat C$ of the observable calibration and test data
such that $\inf_{P\in\mathcal{Q}}\mathbb{P}_P\!\big(\theta_{n+1}\in\widehat
C\big)\ge 1-\alpha$ with $\mathbb{E}_P[\mathrm{Leb}(\widehat C)]<\infty$
uniformly.
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
Two laws $P_0,P_1\in\mathcal{Q}$ can share an identical observable marginal law
of $(Y^1,\dots,Y^K)$ while differing in the conditional law of $\theta$: take a
factor-aligned common-mode reallocation $\varepsilon^c\mapsto
\varepsilon^c+\beta_c\eta,\ \theta\mapsto\theta-\eta$ for an independent
$\eta$ (the $\Delta_\parallel$ direction of \S4). This leaves
\eqref{eq:factor} and the joint law of all $Y^c$ invariant for every $K$ but
shifts the law of $\theta_{n+1}$ by $\eta$ of arbitrary variance. Any $\widehat
C$ that is a function of the observables is identical under $P_0,P_1$; making
its miscoverage $\le\alpha$ under both forces $\mathrm{Leb}(\widehat
C)\to\infty$ as $\mathrm{Var}(\eta)\to\infty$.
\end{proof}

Proposition~\ref{prop:impossible} is why the OICC guarantee is stated as
\emph{coverage up to an estimable deconvolution gap plus a budgeted dependence
term}, and why a single irreducible knob $\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$ must survive.
Everything below is the constructive complement: we cover the identified
(deconvolved) estimand exactly up to estimable error, and we make the residual
to the true latent explicit.

\subsection{Assumptions for the conformal layer}

\begin{assumption}[Conditional independence]\label{as:ci}
$\varepsilon^1,\dots,\varepsilon^K$ are mutually independent given $(\theta,X)$.
\end{assumption}
\begin{assumption}[Pivot regularity]\label{as:pivot}
The pivot error is classical after covariate conditioning:
$\mathbb{E}[\varepsilon^1\mid\theta,X]=0$ and $\varphi_{\varepsilon^1}(t)\ne 0$
for all $t$. (If the pivot is nonclassical, replace mean-zero by
$\mathrm{median}(\varepsilon^1\mid\theta,X)=0$ and route \S3 through
Hu--Schennach; Theorems~\ref{thm:oracle}--\ref{thm:sens} then hold with median
scores in place of mean-centered scores.)
\end{assumption}
\begin{assumption}[Deconvolution identification]\label{as:decon}
Kotlarski/Hu--Schennach regularity of \S3 holds: the error CFs are
non-vanishing (classical) or the conditional-density operator is boundedly
complete (nonclassical), so that $f_{\varepsilon^1}$ and $f_\theta$ are
point-identified from channels $\{2,\dots,K\}$.
\end{assumption}
\begin{assumption}[Calibration exchangeability]\label{as:exch}
Conditional on the fitted model, the pairs $(Z_i)_{i\in\mathcal{I}_{\mathrm{cal}}\cup\{n+1\}}$
are exchangeable, where $Z_i$ collects the latent residual $S_i$ defined below.
(Relaxed to weighted/blocked exchangeability in \S\ref{sec:panel}.)
\end{assumption}
\begin{assumption}[Bounded dependence ball]\label{as:ball}
The off-diagonal error covariance splits orthogonally as
$\Delta=\Delta_\perp\oplus\Delta_\parallel$ with detectable part
$\|\Delta_\perp\|\le\tau$ and factor-aligned part
$\Delta_\parallel=\gamma\,\beta\beta^\top,\ \|\Delta_\parallel\|\le\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$.
\end{assumption}

\subsection{The latent-scale nonconformity score}
\label{sec:score}

Let $m(\cdot)$ be a plug-in latent predictor built from the
\emph{leave-the-pivot-out} channels only,
\begin{equation}
\hat\theta_i \;=\; m\big(Y^2_i,\dots,Y^K_i\big),
\qquad
m(\cdot)=\mathbb{E}_{\hat f_\theta,\hat f_{\varepsilon^{2:K}}}\!\big[\theta\mid Y^{2:K}\big]
\ \text{(empirical-Bayes deconvolution posterior mean).}
\label{eq:predictor}
\end{equation}
Define the \emph{infeasible latent residual} and the \emph{feasible pivot residual}
\begin{equation}
S_i \;=\; \theta_i-\hat\theta_i \quad(\text{unobserved}),
\qquad
R_i \;=\; Y^1_i-\hat\theta_i \;=\; S_i+\varepsilon^1_i \quad(\text{observed}).
\label{eq:scores}
\end{equation}
The score at the latent scale is $|S_i|$; it is \emph{not} computable, but its
\emph{law} is identified. Under Assumptions~\ref{as:ci}--\ref{as:pivot} and
because $\hat\theta_i$ uses only channels $\{2,\dots,K\}$, $\varepsilon^1_i$ is
independent of $S_i$ (given $X$), hence
\begin{equation}
F_R \;=\; F_S * F_{\varepsilon^1}
\qquad\Longleftrightarrow\qquad
\varphi_S(t)\;=\;\frac{\varphi_R(t)}{\varphi_{\varepsilon^1}(t)} .
\label{eq:deconv-identity}
\end{equation}
Equation~\eqref{eq:deconv-identity} is the crux: \emph{the pivot residual $R_i$
is a computable nonconformity score whose law is the latent-residual law
convolved with the identified pivot error}. Deconvolving by
$f_{\varepsilon^1}$ recovers the score law we cannot observe.

\begin{definition}[OICC prediction set]\label{def:set}
Let $\hat F_S$ be the regularized deconvolution of the empirical law of
$\{R_i\}$ by $\hat f_{\varepsilon^1}$,
\begin{equation}
\hat\varphi_S(t)\;=\;\frac{\widehat{\varphi}_R(t)\,\overline{\hat\varphi_{\varepsilon^1}(t)}}
{|\hat\varphi_{\varepsilon^1}(t)|^2+\varrho_n(t)}\,K_h(t),
\qquad
\widehat{\varphi}_R(t)=\tfrac1n\!\sum_{i}e^{\mathrm{i}tR_i},
\label{eq:reg-deconv}
\end{equation}
with Tikhonov ridge $\varrho_n(t)$ and CF-kernel $K_h$ (bandwidth $h$). Let
$\hat q_{\alpha/2},\hat q_{1-\alpha/2}$ be the corresponding quantiles of
$\hat F_S$. The set is
\begin{equation}
\widehat C^{\theta}_\alpha(i)\;=\;\big[\,\hat\theta_i+\hat q_{\alpha/2}\,,\ \hat\theta_i+\hat q_{1-\alpha/2}\,\big].
\label{eq:base-set}
\end{equation}
\end{definition}

\subsection{Coverage of the deconvolved estimand}

We first isolate what is covered \emph{exactly} in the oracle-deconvolution
limit, then pay the finite-sample and estimation costs.

\begin{theorem}[Oracle-deconvolution coverage]\label{thm:oracle}
Under Assumptions~\ref{as:ci}--\ref{as:exch} with $\Delta_\perp=\Delta_\parallel=0$,
if $\hat f_{\varepsilon^1}=f_{\varepsilon^1}$ and $\hat F_S=F_S$, then
$\mathbb{P}\big(\theta_{n+1}\in\widehat C^{\theta}_\alpha\big)\ge 1-\alpha$,
with equality up to the atom correction $1/(n+1)$ under split conformal.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
$\theta_{n+1}\in\widehat C^\theta_\alpha$ iff $S_{n+1}\in[\hat q_{\alpha/2},\hat
q_{1-\alpha/2}]$. Under exact deconvolution these are the true $F_S$ quantiles;
by exchangeability of $\{S_i\}\cup\{S_{n+1}\}$ (Assumption~\ref{as:exch}) the
rank of $S_{n+1}$ among the calibration residuals is uniform, giving the split-
conformal bound $\ge 1-\lceil(1-\alpha)(n+1)\rceil/(n+1)+\text{ties}\ge1-\alpha$.
Independence of $\varepsilon^1$ from $S$ (Assumptions~\ref{as:ci}--\ref{as:pivot})
is what makes \eqref{eq:deconv-identity} exact, so the deconvolved quantiles are
the correct latent-residual quantiles.
\end{proof}

\begin{theorem}[Feasible split-conformal coverage of the deconvolved latent]\label{thm:feasible}
Under Assumptions~\ref{as:ci}--\ref{as:exch} with $\Delta_\perp=\Delta_\parallel=0$,
using the estimated $\hat f_{\varepsilon^1}$ and $n$ calibration units, for any
$\eta\in(0,1)$, with probability $\ge 1-\eta$ over the calibration draw,
\begin{equation}
\mathbb{P}\big(\theta_{n+1}\in\widehat C^\theta_\alpha\big)
\;\ge\;
1-\alpha
\;-\;\underbrace{2\,\|\hat F_S-F_S\|_\infty}_{=:2\xi_n}
\;-\;\tfrac{1}{n+1},
\label{eq:feasible-bound}
\end{equation}
and the deconvolution error decomposes as
\begin{equation}
\xi_n
\;\le\;
\underbrace{\big\|\mathcal{D}_{f_{\varepsilon^1}}\big\|_{\mathrm{op}}\cdot
\Big(\tfrac{\log(2/\eta)}{2n}\Big)^{1/2}}_{\text{sampling of }F_R,\ \text{DKW}\times\text{ill-posed amplification}}
\;+\;
\underbrace{L_{\mathcal D}\,\big\|\hat f_{\varepsilon^1}-f_{\varepsilon^1}\big\|_{\ast}}_{\text{pivot-error estimation}} ,
\label{eq:xi-decomp}
\end{equation}
where $\mathcal{D}_{f_{\varepsilon^1}}$ is the (regularized) deconvolution
operator of \eqref{eq:reg-deconv}, $L_{\mathcal D}$ its Lipschitz modulus in the
error law, and $\|\cdot\|_\ast$ the CF-weighted norm. In particular:
\begin{itemize}
\item[(i)] \emph{Ordinary-smooth pivot error} of order $p$
($|\varphi_{\varepsilon^1}(t)|\asymp|t|^{-p}$):
$\xi_n=O_P\!\big(n^{-\frac{\kappa}{2\kappa+2p+1}}\big)$ for $\kappa$-smooth $F_S$;
\item[(ii)] \emph{Supersmooth pivot error} (e.g.\ Gaussian recall noise,
$|\varphi_{\varepsilon^1}(t)|\asymp e^{-\gamma|t|^{r}}$):
$\xi_n=O_P\!\big((\log n)^{-\kappa/r}\big)$ --- the honest logarithmic rate.
\end{itemize}
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
Coverage of a plug-in-quantile interval degrades by at most twice the sup-norm
CDF error: if $\|\hat F_S-F_S\|_\infty\le\xi$ then
$F_S(\hat q_{1-\alpha/2})-F_S(\hat q_{\alpha/2})\ge (1-\alpha/2-\xi)-(\alpha/2+\xi)=1-\alpha-2\xi$,
and the split-conformal atom costs $1/(n+1)$; combine with
Theorem~\ref{thm:oracle}. For \eqref{eq:xi-decomp}, write
$\hat F_S-F_S=\mathcal{D}_{\hat f_{\varepsilon^1}}[\widehat F_R]-\mathcal{D}_{f_{\varepsilon^1}}[F_R]$
and add-subtract $\mathcal{D}_{f_{\varepsilon^1}}[\widehat F_R]$. The first term
is the operator applied to the empirical process $\widehat F_R-F_R$, bounded in
sup-norm by $\|\mathcal D\|_{\mathrm{op}}$ times the DKW deviation
$(\log(2/\eta)/2n)^{1/2}$; the second is the operator's sensitivity to the plugged
error law, Lipschitz with modulus $L_{\mathcal D}$. Rates (i)--(ii) are the
standard deconvolution-density sup-norm rates (Fan 1991; Meister 2009)
transferred to the CDF/quantile via the regularized ridge in
\eqref{eq:reg-deconv}.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[What Theorem~\ref{thm:feasible} covers, precisely]
The set covers the \emph{deconvolved estimand}: the $(1-\alpha)$ predictive
interval of $\theta_{n+1}$ under the \emph{identified} model
$(\hat f_\theta,\hat f_{\varepsilon^{1:K}})$. The $\|\mathcal
D\|_{\mathrm{op}}$ factor is where the missing label is paid for: it is finite
only under the smoothness class that makes deconvolution well-posed, and it
\emph{amplifies} the DKW sampling error --- this is the quantitative content of
Proposition~\ref{prop:impossible}. There is no distribution-free finite-sample
$1-\alpha$; the ill-posedness tax is unavoidable.
\end{remark}

\subsection{The extra assumption that upgrades ``deconvolved estimand'' to ``true latent''}

\begin{definition}[Identification-completeness condition]\label{def:IC}
Condition \textsf{(IC)} holds iff the one-factor model \eqref{eq:factor} with
Assumptions~\ref{as:ci}--\ref{as:decon} holds \emph{exactly} \textbf{and}
$\Delta_\parallel=0$ (no factor-aligned common mode), equivalently
$\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}=0$.
\end{definition}

\begin{corollary}[True-latent coverage]\label{cor:true}
Add \textsf{(IC)} to the hypotheses of Theorem~\ref{thm:feasible}. Then the
identified law equals the true law, $\hat F_S\xrightarrow{P}F_S^{\mathrm{true}}$,
and the same bound \eqref{eq:feasible-bound} holds with $\theta_{n+1}$ the
\emph{true} latent rate. Conversely, if $\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}>0$, the covered
target is displaced from the true latent by an unidentifiable location--scale
map of magnitude $O(\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}})$ (Proposition~\ref{prop:impossible}),
recovered only up to the sensitivity term $g(\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}})$ of
Theorem~\ref{thm:sens}.
\end{corollary}

\noindent\textbf{This is the exact, single extra assumption \textsf{(IC)}}: it
is not testable in the $\Delta_\parallel$ direction (Proposition~\ref{prop:impossible}),
it \emph{is} testable in the $\Delta_\perp$ direction (\S\ref{sec:sens}), and it
is the only gap between the two theorems.

\subsection{The methods coupling: over-identification slack $\to$ conformal sensitivity radius}
\label{sec:sens}

We now make the detectable violation drive the interval width. Let
$\{\hat f_\theta^{(jk)}\}_{j<k}$ be the pairwise deconvolution recoveries; under
the maintained model they coincide. Their discrepancy is the over-ID statistic
\begin{equation}
\hat\delta_\perp
\;=\;
\Big(\ \sum_{j<k}\ \big\|\hat f_\theta^{(jk)}-\bar f_\theta\big\|_{L^2}^2\ \Big)^{1/2}
\quad\big(\text{equivalently a Hansen--Sargan/CF $J$-statistic}\big),
\qquad
\hat\delta_\perp\xrightarrow{P}\ \psi\big(\|\Delta_\perp\|\big),
\label{eq:overid}
\end{equation}
with $\psi$ a known, strictly increasing calibration function (identity to first
order in the Gaussian regime below), and $\hat\delta_\perp$ has \emph{zero
population probability limit under any $\Delta_\parallel$} (\S4 impossibility).

\begin{theorem}[Over-ID--calibrated sensitivity inflation; derivation of $f(\tau)$]\label{thm:sens}
Adopt Assumption~\ref{as:ball} with detectable coupling
$\rho:=\mathrm{Cov}(\varepsilon^1,\varepsilon^{2:K})$, $\|\rho\|=\tau$, and let
$w$ be the weights of the linear plug-in predictor $\hat\theta=w^\top Y^{2:K}$
in \eqref{eq:predictor}. Then:
\begin{enumerate}
\item[\rm(a)] \emph{(Bias of the naive deconvolution.)} The independence-assuming
deconvolution mis-estimates the latent-residual scale by exactly
\begin{equation}
\hat v^{\mathrm{naive}}_S
\;=\;
\mathrm{Var}(R)-\sigma_1^2
\;=\;
v_S - 2\,\kappa,
\qquad
\kappa:=w^\top\rho,\qquad |\kappa|\le\|w\|\,\tau
\ \text{(Cauchy--Schwarz)} .
\label{eq:bias}
\end{equation}

\item[\rm(b)] \emph{(Derived inflation.)} Define the sensitivity radius
\begin{equation}
\boxed{\,f(\tau)\;=\;z_{1-\alpha/2}\Big(\sqrt{\hat v^{\mathrm{naive}}_S+2\|w\|\,\tau}\;-\;\sqrt{\hat v^{\mathrm{naive}}_S}\Big)
\;=\;\frac{z_{1-\alpha/2}\,\|w\|}{\sqrt{\hat v^{\mathrm{naive}}_S}}\,\tau\;+\;O(\tau^2).}
\label{eq:ftau}
\end{equation}
In the general (non-Gaussian) case the same worst-case control gives the
quantile-Lipschitz bound
$f(\tau)\le \|w\|\,\tau/\underline{p}$, where
$\underline{p}=\inf_{u\in\mathcal N}f_S(u)$ is the smallest latent-residual
density on the relevant neighborhood $\mathcal N$.

\item[\rm(c)] \emph{(Common-mode term.)} The factor-aligned part inflates the
$S$-scale by at most $\|w\|^2\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$ (it loads through
$\hat\theta=w^\top Y^{2:K}$ and is absorbed into the factor), giving an additive
term with zero data-power:
\begin{equation}
g(\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}})
\;=\;
z_{1-\alpha/2}\Big(\sqrt{\hat v^{\mathrm{naive}}_S+\|w\|^2\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}}-\sqrt{\hat v^{\mathrm{naive}}_S}\Big).
\label{eq:gGamma}
\end{equation}

\item[\rm(d)] \emph{(Calibrated set and its coverage.)} Define
\begin{equation}
\widehat C^{\theta,\Gamma}_\alpha(i)
=\Big[\hat\theta_i+\hat q_{\alpha/2}-f(\hat\delta_\perp)-g(\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}),\
\hat\theta_i+\hat q_{1-\alpha/2}+f(\hat\delta_\perp)+g(\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}})\Big].
\label{eq:final-set}
\end{equation}
Then for any true perturbation with $\|\Delta_\perp\|\le\psi^{-1}(\hat\delta_\perp)(1+o_P(1))$
and $\|\Delta_\parallel\|\le\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$,
\begin{equation}
\mathbb{P}\big(\theta_{n+1}\in\widehat C^{\theta,\Gamma}_\alpha\big)
\;\ge\;
1-\alpha-2\xi_n-\tfrac{1}{n+1}-o_P(1).
\label{eq:sens-coverage}
\end{equation}
\end{enumerate}
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
\emph{(a)} With $\hat\theta=w^\top Y^{2:K}=w^\top(\alpha+\beta\theta+\varepsilon^{2:K})$
and classical pivot ($\mathrm{Cov}(\theta,\varepsilon^1)=0$),
$\mathrm{Cov}(S,\varepsilon^1)=\mathrm{Cov}(\theta,\varepsilon^1)-\mathrm{Cov}(\hat\theta,\varepsilon^1)
=-w^\top\rho=-\kappa$. Hence
$\mathrm{Var}(R)=v_S+\sigma_1^2+2\mathrm{Cov}(S,\varepsilon^1)=v_S+\sigma_1^2-2\kappa$,
and the independence-assuming estimator subtracts only $\sigma_1^2$, giving
$\hat v^{\mathrm{naive}}_S=v_S-2\kappa$, i.e.\ $v_S=\hat v^{\mathrm{naive}}_S+2\kappa$.
Cauchy--Schwarz bounds $|\kappa|\le\|w\|\,\tau$.

\emph{(b)} The true half-width needs $z_{1-\alpha/2}\sqrt{v_S}$. Since
$v_S=\hat v^{\mathrm{naive}}_S+2\kappa\le\hat v^{\mathrm{naive}}_S+2\|w\|\tau$
(worst case $\kappa=+\|w\|\tau$, the under-coverage direction; $\kappa<0$ only
over-covers), replacing $v_S$ by its worst-case upper bound and subtracting the
plug-in half-width $z_{1-\alpha/2}\sqrt{\hat v^{\mathrm{naive}}_S}$ yields
\eqref{eq:ftau}. Linearization $\sqrt{v+\delta}-\sqrt v=\delta/(2\sqrt v)+O(\delta^2)$
gives the slope. Non-Gaussian: the true $(1-\alpha)$ latent-residual quantile
moves from the naive one by at most the density-weighted mass shift; a
$1/\underline p$ inverse-density Lipschitz bound on the quantile map converts the
$\|w\|\tau$ mass perturbation into the stated width bound.

\emph{(c)} $\Delta_\parallel=\gamma\beta\beta^\top$ contributes
$\mathrm{Var}(w^\top\varepsilon^{2:K})$ an extra $w^\top(\gamma\beta\beta^\top)w=\gamma(w^\top\beta)^2$;
with $|\gamma|\|\beta\|^2\le\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$ and $|w^\top\beta|\le\|w\|\|\beta\|$
this is $\le\|w\|^2\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$. It is absorbed into the estimated factor
(Proposition~\ref{prop:impossible}), so $\hat\delta_\perp$ has no power against it;
inflating the $S$-scale by this amount conservatively restores coverage, giving
\eqref{eq:gGamma}.

\emph{(d)} Combine: under the worst admissible $(\Delta_\perp,\Delta_\parallel)$ in
the ball, the true latent-residual quantiles lie inside the inflated interval
except on the deconvolution/sampling event of probability
$\le 2\xi_n+\tfrac{1}{n+1}$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:feasible}); the plug-in of
$\hat\delta_\perp$ for $\tau$ costs $o_P(1)$ by consistency of \eqref{eq:overid}.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Why the coupling is the new object]
$f$ is not a user knob: its argument is the \emph{data-estimated}
$\hat\delta_\perp$, so the interval \emph{widens exactly where the over-ID
tripwire fires} --- the high-disparity cells --- and coverage degrades visibly
rather than silently. $g$ \emph{is} a knob, but only on the provably
data-blind $\Delta_\parallel$ direction, and \S4's lower bound certifies it
cannot be shrunk by any $K$. The pair $(f(\hat\delta_\perp),g(\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}))$
is the ``robustness radius that is data-driven on the identifiable subspace and
provably-minimal user-specified on the complement'' claimed as the paper's net
novelty. Note the direction the naive method is blind to (\S4) is precisely the
one where $f\equiv 0$: $f$ can only ever inflate for $\Delta_\perp$, never
$\Delta_\parallel$ --- we state this rather than hide it.
\end{remark}

\subsection{Panel exchangeability: weighted/blocked conformal}
\label{sec:panel}

Assumption~\ref{as:exch} fails on panels: (i) area--period units are temporally
dependent, and (ii) the test period $T{+}1$ is a forecasting target, i.e.\ a
covariate/time shift relative to calibration. We repair both.

\begin{theorem}[Weighted, block-thinned conformal coverage]\label{thm:weighted}
Replace the calibration empirical CDF of $R$ by the tilted CDF with normalized
weights $p_i=w(Z_i)/\sum_{j}w(Z_j)$, $w(z)=\mathrm{d}\mathbb{P}_{T+1}/\mathrm{d}\mathbb{P}_{\mathrm{cal}}(z)$
(covariate/time-shift likelihood ratio; Tibshirani et al.\ 2019), and thin the
calibration index into blocks separated by lag $\ell$. Then
\begin{equation}
\mathbb{P}\big(\theta_{n+1}\in\widehat C^{\theta,\Gamma}_\alpha\big)
\;\ge\;
1-\alpha
\;-\;2\xi_n
\;-\;\underbrace{2\,\mathrm{TV}(\hat w,w)}_{\text{weight misspecification}}
\;-\;\underbrace{2\,\beta_{\mathrm{mix}}(\ell)}_{\text{temporal dependence}}
\;-\;\tfrac{1}{n_{\mathrm{blk}}+1},
\label{eq:weighted-bound}
\end{equation}
where $\beta_{\mathrm{mix}}(\ell)$ is the $\beta$-mixing coefficient of the
per-area residual process at gap $\ell$ and $n_{\mathrm{blk}}$ the number of
$\ell$-separated blocks. If instead one adopts online/adaptive conformal
(Gibbs--Cand\`es 2021) over the time index, the guarantee becomes the
distribution-free long-run bound
$\big|\tfrac1T\sum_{t\le T}\mathbf 1\{\theta_t\notin\widehat C_t\}-\alpha\big|\le C/(\gamma_{\text{lr}} T)$
with no exchangeability requirement, at the cost of only time-averaged
(not pointwise) validity.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
Weighted-conformal validity under covariate shift gives $1-\alpha$ exactly when
$w$ is known (Tibshirani et al.\ 2019, Thm.\ 2); plugging $\hat w$ costs
$2\,\mathrm{TV}(\hat w,w)$ by the coupling inequality on the tilted rank.
Coupling the dependent block sequence to an i.i.d.\ surrogate costs
$2\beta_{\mathrm{mix}}(\ell)$ per Berbee's lemma; exchangeability then holds
across the $n_{\mathrm{blk}}$ near-independent blocks, giving the split-conformal
atom $1/(n_{\mathrm{blk}}+1)$. Add the deconvolution term $2\xi_n$ of
Theorem~\ref{thm:feasible}. The online alternative is Gibbs--Cand\`es adaptive
conformal, whose regret bound is distribution-free.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Spatial clustering]
For the India state--year field ($\sim$34--36 areas $\times$ 14 yrs), block by
\emph{area} (Kluger et al.\ 2025 spatial/clustered PPI): the exchangeable unit is
the area trajectory, and $\beta_{\mathrm{mix}}$ is replaced by the spatial
dependence range. The estimand's resolution ceiling (state--year, \S3) is
inherited here: $n_{\mathrm{blk}}$ is small, so \eqref{eq:weighted-bound}'s block
atom $1/(n_{\mathrm{blk}}+1)$ is non-negligible and must be reported, not hidden.
\end{remark}

\subsection{Deconvolution error propagation into the score (hostile pre-emption)}

The score $R_i$ is exact, but the \emph{score law} $\hat F_S$ inherits the
ill-posed inverse. Two propagation channels, both already budgeted:
\emph{(1) plugged pivot-error law} --- $\|\hat f_{\varepsilon^1}-f_{\varepsilon^1}\|_\ast$
enters through $L_{\mathcal D}$ in \eqref{eq:xi-decomp}; if $f_{\varepsilon^1}$ is
itself only identified up to \S3's over-ID slack, its estimation error is
\emph{correlated} with $\hat\delta_\perp$, so we bound them jointly: replace
$\xi_n$ by $\xi_n+L_{\mathcal D}\,c\,\hat\delta_\perp$ (a cross-term folded into
$f(\hat\delta_\perp)$'s slope). \emph{(2) operator amplification} --- for
supersmooth pivot error the sampling error is amplified logarithmically
(Theorem~\ref{thm:feasible}(ii)); we therefore \emph{never} claim $\sqrt n$
coverage rates for a Gaussian-recall pivot and instead report the
$(\log n)^{-\kappa/r}$ envelope explicitly. The interval is honest precisely
because $\xi_n$ appears \emph{outside} the nominal $1-\alpha$ in every theorem.

\subsection{Finite-sample slack (hostile pre-emption)}

Every theorem carries three explicit finite-sample debits, none absorbed into
$o_P(1)$ asymptotics without a stated rate:
\begin{itemize}
\item split-conformal atom $1/(n+1)$ (or $1/(n_{\mathrm{blk}}+1)$ on panels),
\item DKW sampling term $\|\mathcal D\|_{\mathrm{op}}\sqrt{\log(2/\eta)/2n}$,
scaled by the ill-posedness operator norm,
\item over-ID plug-in slack: $\hat\delta_\perp$ is an estimate; use its one-sided
$(1-\beta)$ upper confidence bound $\hat\delta_\perp^{+}$ in
\eqref{eq:final-set}, which adds the $\beta$-level to the miscoverage budget
exactly as in \S5's Theorem, giving the fully-honest bound
\begin{equation}
\mathbb{P}\big(\theta_{n+1}\in\widehat C^{\theta,\Gamma}_\alpha\big)
\;\ge\;
1-\alpha-2\xi_n-2\,\mathrm{TV}(\hat w,w)-2\beta_{\mathrm{mix}}(\ell)-\beta-\tfrac{1}{n_{\mathrm{blk}}+1}.
\label{eq:master}
\end{equation}
\end{itemize}
Equation~\eqref{eq:master} is the master coverage statement: nominal $1-\alpha$,
minus deconvolution error ($2\xi_n$, estimable with stated rate), minus panel
corrections ($\mathrm{TV}$, $\beta_{\mathrm{mix}}$), minus the over-ID confidence
level $\beta$, minus the block atom. The unidentifiable $\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$
does \emph{not} appear as a debit --- it is paid in \emph{width} via
$g(\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}})$, which is the correct accounting: an unfalsifiable
assumption must cost interval length, never claimed coverage.

\subsection{Codeable form}

\begin{align}
&\textbf{scores:} && R_i = Y^1_i - m(Y^{2:K}_i),\quad i\in\mathcal I_{\mathrm{cal}} \\
&\textbf{empirical CF:} && \widehat\varphi_R(t)=\tfrac1n\textstyle\sum_i e^{\mathrm i t R_i} \\
&\textbf{regularized deconv:} && \hat\varphi_S(t)=\dfrac{\widehat\varphi_R(t)\,\overline{\hat\varphi_{\varepsilon^1}(t)}}{|\hat\varphi_{\varepsilon^1}(t)|^2+\varrho_n(t)}\,K_h(t),\quad
\hat f_S=\mathcal F^{-1}[\hat\varphi_S] \\
&\textbf{latent quantiles:} && \hat q_u=\hat F_S^{-1}(u),\quad u\in\{\alpha/2,\,1-\alpha/2\} \\
&\textbf{over-ID slack:} && \hat\delta_\perp=\Big(\sum_{j<k}\|\hat f_\theta^{(jk)}-\bar f_\theta\|_{L^2}^2\Big)^{1/2},\ \ \hat\delta_\perp^{+}=\hat\delta_\perp+z_{1-\beta}\,\widehat{\mathrm{se}}(\hat\delta_\perp)\\
&\textbf{inflation:} && f(\hat\delta_\perp^{+})=z_{1-\alpha/2}\big(\sqrt{\hat v_S^{\mathrm{naive}}+2\|w\|\hat\delta_\perp^{+}}-\sqrt{\hat v_S^{\mathrm{naive}}}\big),\ \
g=z_{1-\alpha/2}\big(\sqrt{\hat v_S^{\mathrm{naive}}+\|w\|^2\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}}-\sqrt{\hat v_S^{\mathrm{naive}}}\big)\\
&\textbf{set:} && \widehat C^{\theta,\Gamma}_\alpha(i)=\hat\theta_i+\big[\hat q_{\alpha/2}-f-g,\ \hat q_{1-\alpha/2}+f+g\big]
\end{align}
with weights $p_i\propto \hat w(Z_i)$ replacing $\tfrac1n$ in $\widehat\varphi_R$
under the panel repair of \S\ref{sec:panel}, and $\hat v_S^{\mathrm{naive}}=\mathrm{Var}(R)-\hat\sigma_1^2$.
```

---

## Report on what I produced and the load-bearing honesty checks

I wrote the complete, compile-ready LaTeX section for the OICC conformal layer. It delivers every requested component and, per the mandate, contains no circular or false claim that survives scrutiny. Key design decisions and findings:

**The central construction (the genuinely new object).** The missing-label trap is real: $\theta$ is never observed, so no latent score exists. I resolved it with a **leave-the-pivot-out residual** $R_i = Y^1_i - m(Y^{2:K}_i) = S_i + \varepsilon^1_i$ — a *computable* nonconformity score whose *law* is the (infeasible) latent-residual law convolved with the identified pivot error. Deconvolving $R$'s law by $f_{\varepsilon^1}$ recovers the latent-residual quantiles. This makes §5's one-line sketch ("conformalize $Y^1$, then deconvolve the set") mathematically precise, and it corrects a naive reading of that sketch (you cannot just subtract $\varepsilon^1$ from a $Y^1$-interval; the error is random).

**Impossibility stated as a theorem, not hidden (Prop. 4.1).** Two laws differing only in the factor-aligned direction share every observable marginal for all $K$ but move $\theta$ arbitrarily — so finite-sample distribution-free latent coverage is provably impossible. This is what forces the guarantee to be "coverage up to an estimable gap," and it is the same $\Delta_\parallel$ object that caps the program.

**The methods coupling, with $f(\tau)$ actually derived (Thm 4.9).** In the Gaussian first-order regime I derive that the independence-assuming deconvolution biases the latent-residual variance by exactly $-2\kappa$, $\kappa = w^\top\rho$, $|\kappa|\le\|w\|\tau$. Restoring worst-case coverage gives the boxed radius $f(\tau)=z_{1-\alpha/2}(\sqrt{\hat v+2\|w\|\tau}-\sqrt{\hat v})$, linear in $\tau$ with slope $z\|w\|/\sqrt{\hat v}$; the general case gets a reciprocal-density Lipschitz bound $f(\tau)\le\|w\|\tau/\underline p$. Crucially, the argument is the *data-estimated* $\hat\delta_\perp$, so the interval widens exactly where the over-ID tripwire fires. I verified the sign: under-coverage occurs when $\kappa>0$, so inflating variance by $2\|w\|\tau$ is the correct one-sided worst case.

**The exact extra assumption for TRUE latent (Def. 4.5 / Cor. 4.6).** Condition **(IC)**: the one-factor CI model holds exactly *and* $\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}=0$. It is the sole gap between the deconvolved estimand and the true latent, untestable in the $\Delta_\parallel$ direction, testable in the $\Delta_\perp$ direction — matching the program's "provably-minimal residual" thesis. The common-mode cost is paid in **width** ($g(\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}})$), never in claimed coverage.

**All three hostile pre-emptions are in-text and budgeted, not waved away:**
- *Panel exchangeability failure* → weighted conformal (covariate/time-shift LR) + $\beta$-mixing block-thinning (Berbee), plus a Gibbs–Candès online fallback for distribution-free time-averaged validity; the small $n_{\mathrm{blk}}$ from the India state-year ceiling is flagged as a non-negligible atom.
- *Deconvolution error into the score* → decomposed into DKW-sampling × ill-posedness operator norm plus pivot-error estimation, with honest rates: ordinary-smooth $n^{-\kappa/(2\kappa+2p+1)}$, supersmooth (Gaussian recall) the painful $(\log n)^{-\kappa/r}$. I explicitly refuse to claim $\sqrt n$ rates for Gaussian pivot error.
- *Finite-sample slack* → the master bound (Eq. 4-master) carries the split atom $1/(n_{\mathrm{blk}}+1)$, DKW term, weight-TV, mixing, and the over-ID confidence level $\beta$ (via a one-sided UCB $\hat\delta_\perp^+$), all *outside* the nominal $1-\alpha$.

**Codeable equations** are provided as a compact block (empirical CF → Tikhonov-regularized deconvolution → latent quantiles → over-ID slack with UCB → inflation → set).

One caveat I want to surface for the director: the decomposition "over-ID power $\propto\|\Delta_\perp\|$, zero on $\Delta_\parallel$" and the $f(\tau)$ derivation are given rigorously in the **Gaussian/first-order (classical-pivot) regime**; the general nonclassical Hu–Schennach regime is covered only by the weaker Lipschitz bound $f(\tau)\le\|w\|\tau/\underline p$ and a stated median-score substitution. That is exactly the "state it as a local/first-order result if that is all that holds" fallback §8 anticipated — the primitive stands, but the fully nonclassical exact version is not proven here and remains the load-bearing proof obligation (i) for the build.

This section is theorem-first (not pipeline-first), which is the property §8 identifies as the ceiling-breaking one.
