# OICC INDIA CROSS-NATIONAL INTEGRATION SPEC (verified on disk)

## 0. What the data actually is (verified, not assumed)

All paths under `C:/Users/kamle/OneDrive/Desktop/crime-detection-ai/data/`.

**Two incompatible geographic resolutions confirmed:**
- **District × year** exists ONLY in the `crime/01_District_wise_*` police-record series: `crime/01_District_wise_crimes_committed_IPC_2001_2012.csv` (2001–2012), `..._2013.csv`, `..._2014.csv`. 35 STATE/UT, contains aggregate rows `TOTAL` and `DELHI UT TOTAL` that MUST be filtered. Note the 2014 file has a **completely different, expanded schema** (90+ columns, split rape/riot subheads) and lower-cased `States/UTs` — it needs a separate harmonization map; do not `pd.concat` blindly.
- **State × year** for EVERY bias/independence channel. Verified: `crime/27_Nature_of_complaints_received_by_police.csv`, `25_Complaints_against_police.csv`, `35_Human_rights_violation_by_police.csv`, `40_01..05_Custodial_death_*.csv` are ALL keyed `Area_Name, Year` at state level (33–35 areas). There is **no district key anywhere in these channels.**

**This kills any district-level multi-channel deconvolution.** The identification estimand for India is **STATE × YEAR**, exactly as the core doc declared. District IPC can enter only as a downscaling/aux covariate, never as a channel.

**Temporal ceiling — the hard, previously-understated constraint:** every independent channel (25, 27, 35, 40_*) covers **2001–2010 only** (verified: `sort -u` on Year yields exactly 2001..2010). District IPC records run 2001–2014. **The multi-channel identification window is therefore 2001–2010, ≈ 10 years.** 2011–2014 police records exist but have NO contemporaneous independent channel, so they are usable only for out-of-window forecasting/monitoring, not identification.

**Panel size, honest count:** ~35 states × 10 years ≈ **≤350 state-year cells** (27 has 349 usable rows; 34 states in 2001, 35 thereafter; custodial 40_01 has 33 areas). This is the real N for the India deconvolution. It is small — this bounds every asymptotic claim (see §6).

---

## 1. Channel assignment — by GENERATING MECHANISM, not by file

The core doc's channel-selection rule ("select by generating mechanism; police records/arrests/cases collapse to ONE institutional filter") is the binding constraint. Applied to the verified tables:

| Channel | India table(s) | Column(s) | Generating mechanism / filter | Independent? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **C1 — Police records (the biased pivot $Y^1$)** | `crime/01_District_wise_...` → **aggregate district→state**, col `TOTAL IPC CRIMES` (2001–13) / `Total Cognizable IPC crimes` (2014) | count of FIR-registered cognizable IPC crime | Police-registration filter (burking / FIR refusal) | This IS the bias source — the pivot, not independent |
| **C2 — Public-initiated distress signal (911-equivalent)** | `crime/27_Nature_of_complaints_received_by_police.csv` | `PC3_Distress_call_over_phoneNo_100_etc` | Citizen dials 100 before any police discretion | **Yes** — pre-registration, victim/public-initiated |
| **C3 — Police-accountability / state-violence channel** | `35_Human_rights_violation_by_police.csv` (`Cases_Registered..HR_Total Violations by Police`) + `40_01..05_Custodial_death_*` (`CD_Deaths_Reported`, sum all 5 files) + `25_Complaints_against_police.csv` (`CPA_-_Complaints_Received/Alleged`) | counts of police misconduct / custodial death / complaints against police | Oversight/NHRC/judicial filter — a DIFFERENT institution than the recording police unit | **Yes**, and error is *anti-correlated* with C1 (see §3) |
| **Aux (NOT a channel) — arrests-vs-cases clearance** | `crime/04_02_Person_arrested_..._IPC_2012/13/14` (`Persons arrested during the year_Total`); `42_Cases..women` vs `43_Arrests..women` | arrest/charge-sheet throughput | Same institutional filter as C1 (post-registration police action) → collapses to C1 | **No** — used as a covariate $X$, never a channel |

**Critical modeling decision forced by the mechanism rule:** C1 (records), arrests (04_02), cases-vs-arrests (42/43), clearance — all share the *police institution* filter. They are **ONE** channel, not four. India therefore natively supplies **K = 3 mechanism-independent channels max**: {C1 police-records pivot, C2 distress-call, C3 accountability}. That is exactly the minimum the theorem needs (K≥3), with **zero redundancy** — which is a real fragility to flag, not paper over (§6).

**C3 sub-construction:** combine 25/35/40 into one accountability index rather than treating them as 3 channels — they share the oversight-institution filter (complaints→enquiry→custodial-death are the same pipeline) and would violate conditional independence among themselves. Recommended: C3 = log of the NHRC/custodial composite = `HR_Total Violations + Σ CD_Deaths_Reported(40_01..05) + CPA_Complaints_Received`, per state-year. One channel, one filter.

---

## 2. The estimand and the one-factor model on India data

Per state $a$, year $t$, latent log-victimization $\theta_{at}$:
- $Y^1_{at} = \alpha_1 + \theta_{at} + \varepsilon^1$  (C1, log state-total IPC records; $\beta_1{=}1$ normalization)
- $Y^2_{at} = \alpha_2 + \beta_2\theta_{at} + \varepsilon^2$  (C2, log PC3 distress calls)
- $Y^3_{at} = \alpha_3 + \beta_3\theta_{at} + \varepsilon^3$  (C3, log accountability composite)

All on `log(1+count)` per-capita (need state population — NOT in this repo; must join Census 2001/2011 interpolated state population, flag as an external dependency). Nonclassical Hu–Schennach backbone: $\varepsilon^1$ (police under-recording) is correlated with $\theta$ — burking rises where true crime/disorder is high — so classical Kotlarski is invalid for C1; median-zero normalization on C1.

**Load-bearing caveat that must appear (from core §3):** with *exactly* K=3, the one-factor model is **just-identified in second moments** (Cov(Yⱼ,Yₖ)=βⱼβₖσ²_θ: 3 equations, 3 unknowns, 0 df). India's entire over-identifying content lives in **higher-order cumulants / the full joint CF**, NOT the covariance tetrads. With N≤350 and 10 years, third-cumulant estimates are noisy — this is the single biggest technical risk for the India arm. The over-ID $J$-test (§4) must be built on cumulant moments and reported with its finite-sample power caveat, or a factor-analysis referee sinks it.

---

## 3. Why India is the cross-national identification asset: DIFFERENT bias direction

This is the impact multiplier and it is *structurally real in this data*, not rhetorical:

- **US bias regime:** victim-side non-reporting. Records UNDER-count because victims don't call. Error $\varepsilon^1_{US}$ correlates with victim mistrust / immigrant share. NCVS survey is the independent anchor.
- **India bias regime:** police-side suppression (burking, FIR refusal). Records UNDER-count because police decline to register. Error $\varepsilon^1_{IN}$ correlates with police impunity.

**The verified anti-correlation structure that substitutes for India's missing survey:** the C3 accountability channel's error is *anti-correlated* with C1's error. States where police suppress FIRs (C1 biased down hardest) are precisely the states with the most custodial deaths / HR-violations / complaints (C3 biased UP). This is a genuinely different — and *stronger* — independence structure than the US, because the two channels' errors point in opposite directions rather than sharing a common victim-mistrust confound. A common-mode confound that co-loads C1 and C3 in the *same* direction (the unfalsifiable $\Delta_\parallel$) is substantively implausible here precisely because the institutions are adversarial. That is the cleanest available real-world argument that $\Gamma_{cm}$ is small — worth stating, though NOT a proof (§6).

---

## 4. THE cross-national experiment (exact, pre-registered)

**Estimand per country:** deconvolved latent $\hat f_\theta$ + per-cell OICC conformal set $\widehat C^{\theta,\Gamma}_\alpha$, US = metro-month, India = **state-year 2001–2010**.

**E1 — Over-ID tripwire fires in high-disparity cells, in BOTH countries under DIFFERENT mechanisms.**
Compute the pairwise-Kotlarski / cumulant $J$-statistic $\hat\delta_\perp(a,t)$ per cell (core §4 upper bound). Pre-registered prediction: $\hat\delta_\perp$ is LOW in low-disparity cells and FIRES in high-disparity cells in **both** countries.
- India disparity proxy (independent of all 3 channels): **state custodial-death rate per police-strength** — buildable from `40_*` + `crime/12_Police_strength_actual_and_sanctioned.csv`. Falsification: if $\hat\delta_\perp$ does NOT rise with custodial-death disparity, the India arm fails publicly.
- US disparity proxy: immigrant share / mistrust index (Project A).

**E2 — Mechanism-invariance of the record-vs-latent gap.** Define gap $G_{at}=\hat\theta_{at}-Y^1_{at}$ (latent minus records, i.e., estimated under-recording). Pre-registered directional predictions that *differ by country because the mechanisms differ*:
- India: $G_{at}$ correlates POSITIVELY with state custodial-death/HR-violation rate (police-side suppression → more under-recording where police most unaccountable). Cross-check with `25_Complaints_against_police.csv` `CPA_Complaints_Received`.
- US: $G_{at}$ correlates positively with immigrant-share/mistrust (victim-side).
- **The invariance claim:** a single OICC estimator recovers a $G$ that tracks each country's *own, different, independently-measured* bias driver. A country-specific confound CANNOT explain both because the confounds are different (adversarial-institution vs victim-mistrust). This is the "cannot be gamed" robustness test from core §7.

**E3 — Coverage / calibration.** Report empirical coverage of $\widehat C^{\theta,\Gamma}_\alpha$ against held-out $Y^1$ (the only observable), plus the honest gap decomposition from the OICC theorem: TV-deconvolution term + over-ID level $\beta$ + $\Gamma_{cm}$ knob sweep. Show width GROWS in cells where $\hat\delta_\perp$ fires (coverage degrades visibly, not silently). India: leave-one-state-out spatial CV (Kluger-style) because N is tiny and states are the natural cluster.

**E4 — $\Gamma_{cm}$ minimality demonstration.** Sweep $\Gamma_{cm}\in[0,\Gamma_{max}]$; show the identified-set diameter $D(\hat\delta_\perp,\Gamma_{cm})$ and confirm no data statistic moves the $\Delta_\parallel$-component (empirical illustration of core §4 lower bound: perturb C1,C3 with a common-mode rank-one shift $\propto\beta\beta^\top$ and verify the joint law / all cumulants are invariant → over-ID $J$ has zero power against it).

---

## 5. Concrete build steps (data engineering, in order)

1. **Harmonize district IPC → state totals.** Parse `01_..._2001_2012` (drop rows where DISTRICT ∈ {TOTAL, DELHI UT TOTAL}, sum districts per STATE/UT/YEAR on `TOTAL IPC CRIMES`), `..._2013` (same schema, note Title-case state names), `..._2014` (NEW schema → map `Total Cognizable IPC crimes`, lower-case `States/UTs`). Standardize state names to the 27-file's `Area_Name` spelling (e.g. "Odisha" vs "Orissa", "&" vs "and", Delhi UT). Build a canonical state map — this is the #1 join-failure risk.
2. **C2:** load `27`, take `PC3_Distress_call_over_phoneNo_100_etc`. Strip BOM on `Area_Name`. 349/350 rows nonzero (one zero — impute or drop).
3. **C3:** sum `40_01..05` `CD_Deaths_Reported` per state-year (each file ~200 rows, multiple sub-groups → group-sum first); add `35` `HR_Total Violations by Police` rows; add `25` `CPA_Complaints_Received/Alleged`. One composite per state-year.
4. **Population join (EXTERNAL — not in repo):** Census 2001+2011 state population, log-linear interpolate to 2001–2010. Convert all channels to per-capita rates. FLAG as external dependency.
5. **Restrict to the 2001–2010 × ~35-state grid.** ≤350 cells. This is the identification panel.
6. Fit Hu–Schennach one-factor deconvolution (median-zero on C1); compute cumulant over-ID $J$ → $\hat\delta_\perp$ per cell; conformal-wrap via C1 pivot; run E1–E4.

---

## 6. Honest limits (must survive referee — India-specific)

1. **Annual, not monthly.** India channels are yearly. No within-year dynamics; the US arm is metro-*month*. The two countries are NOT at the same temporal resolution — the cross-national claim is about the *bias-gap mechanism*, NOT matched forecasting granularity. State this; don't let a referee "discover" it.
2. **State-level, not district.** Multi-channel identification is impossible below state resolution because NO independent channel has a district key (verified). District records are aux only. India estimand = state-year field, ~35×10.
3. **N ≤ 350 kills asymptotics.** Kotlarski/Hu–Schennach and cumulant over-ID tests are asymptotic; with ≤350 cells and K=3 (just-identified in 2nd moments, over-ID only in noisy higher cumulants), the India $J$-test has **weak finite-sample power**. Report it as a *local/first-order* result (core §8 explicitly permits this) with bootstrap CIs, and lean on the US arm (millions of records) as the identification workhorse. India is the **mechanism-invariance external-validity axis**, not the primary identification engine.
4. **Only 2001–2010 overlap.** The independent channels stop at 2010; 2011–2014 records have no contemporaneous anchor. 10-year window, hard.
5. **Different legal categories.** Indian IPC heads (dowry death, "cruelty by husband", dacoity, custodial rape) do NOT map onto US UCR/NIBRS categories. The latent $\theta$ is "aggregate IPC victimization" in India vs "index crime" in US — NOT the same construct. The cross-national test is legitimate ONLY at the level of the *bias-gap-vs-disparity relationship* (E2 invariance), never a level comparison of rates. Pre-register at that abstraction only.
6. **K=3 with zero redundancy.** India has exactly 3 mechanism-independent channels after the correct institutional collapse. Lose or contaminate one (e.g., if C2 distress calls are police-logged and thus share C1's filter — a real threat, since PC3 is recorded BY police) and identification collapses to K=2 (point-ID, no over-ID test at all). **The C2 independence assumption is the weakest link and must itself be argued** — dialing 100 precedes registration discretion, but the *logging* of the call is a police act. Flag prominently.
7. **$\Delta_\parallel$ still unfalsifiable here too.** The adversarial-institution argument (§3) makes common-mode *substantively* implausible but does NOT prove $\Gamma_{cm}=0$. The core §8 limitation survives unchanged in India; the anti-correlation structure is evidence, not proof.

**Net:** the India arm is a legitimate, verified, buildable **cross-national mechanism-invariance replication** at state-year 2001–2010 resolution — its value is E2/E1 (bias-gap tracks a *different* known disparity driver under a *different* institutional bias regime), not additional identification power. It strengthens external validity exactly as core §7 claims, provided all seven limits above are stated up front. It does not, on its own, lift novelty — the US arm + the two-sided theorem do that; India supplies the "cannot be gamed by a country-specific confound" evidence.

--- INTEGRATION 2 ---
## Bottom line up front

**Honest patentability score: 3.5 / 10** (up from the prior 1–2/10, but not to a place where I'd spend real money without a strategic reason).

The prior verdict — "patent the system, not the theorem" — is exactly right and survives re-examination. The identification/coverage theorems (Kotlarski/Hu–Schennach deconvolution, the conformal bound, the impossibility lemma) are **mathematical concepts and are per se ineligible** under *Gottschalk v. Benson*, *Parker v. Flook*, *Mayo*, and *Alice*. No drafting rescues a theorem. There is **one** genuinely claimable inventive arrangement, and I draft it below, but you should understand it clears the *eligibility* bar only weakly and faces a serious *obviousness/prior-art* problem and a real *freedom-to-operate* problem. The +1.5 bump over the prior score is for the closed-loop coupling being a defensible *arrangement* (BASCOM-style), not because the outlook is good.

---

## 1. Alice two-step, applied honestly

**Step 1 (directed to an abstract idea?).** A claim reciting "ingest data from channels → deconvolve → estimate a latent rate → output an interval" is squarely *Electric Power Group v. Alstom* (Fed. Cir. 2016): collecting data (even from specialized sources), analyzing it with mathematics, and presenting a result is abstract. Specialized *data content* does not save it. This case is the single biggest threat and it maps almost perfectly onto the naive OICC pipeline. Assume Step 1 is lost.

**Step 2 (inventive concept / "significantly more"?).** Survival depends entirely on framing the invention as a **specific technical improvement realized through a concrete actuation**, in the mold of *Diamond v. Diehr* (math controlling a curing process), *McRO v. Bandai* (specific rules producing a technical output), and *BASCOM v. AT&T* (a non-conventional *arrangement* of known parts is itself the inventive concept). The theorem alone cannot be the inventive concept; the *unconventional closed loop* can be.

**The hard truth about the actuation.** The natural OICC actuation — "allocate patrols / route officers" — is itself a *method of organizing human activity*, an independent abstract-idea bucket. So you **cannot** lean on "dispatch police" as the saving technical element; that makes it *more* abstract, not less. The actuation that saves the claim must be **technical and machine-directed**: (a) gating an automated downstream estimator, (b) triggering acquisition of an additional measurement channel / small-area-estimation subroutine, (c) suppressing or widening an automated alert whose false-alarm rate is a measurable system property. Frame the output as a *self-correcting measurement/monitoring system* whose technical improvement is **calibrated reliability that degrades observably rather than silently** — not as a policing decision aid.

---

## 2. The one inventive concept worth claiming

Everything defensible reduces to a single arrangement that, as far as the literature shows, **does not exist as a coupled system**:

> A closed-loop estimator in which a **cross-channel over-identification discrepancy statistic** (computed from ≥3 mechanism-independent measurement channels of one latent process) **directly gates**, per spatial unit, both (i) the **width of a conformal prediction set** and (ii) a **downstream automated actuation**, such that when the discrepancy exceeds a computed one-sided bound the system widens the set *and* diverts to a fallback acquisition/estimation path.

The novelty is the *feedback wiring*: specification-test statistic → conformal inflation → actuation gate, per unit. Every component is individually known (conformal prediction: Vovk; deconvolution: Kotlarski; over-ID/Hansen–Sargan: known; sensitivity radii: Jin–Ren–Candès). The *arrangement*, and specifically making the robustness radius **data-driven from the over-ID residual rather than a user knob**, is the BASCOM-style non-conventional combination. That is the only thing I would try to protect.

---

## 3. Draft claims (2 system, 1 method) — drafted to dodge §101

**Claim 1 (independent, system).**
A monitoring system comprising:
- one or more data-ingestion interfaces configured to receive, for each of a plurality of spatial units and time periods, count or rate signals from **at least three measurement channels that are institutionally mechanism-independent**, the channels comprising at least one administrative-record channel, at least one public-initiated-contact channel, and at least one independent-oversight channel;
- a deconvolution module configured to estimate, from the joint characteristic function of the channel signals, (a) a per-channel error law and (b) a latent-rate residual distribution for a pivot channel;
- an **over-identification module** configured to compute, per spatial unit, a discrepancy statistic between pairwise latent-rate reconstructions and a one-sided upper confidence bound thereon;
- a conformal module configured to construct, per spatial unit, a prediction set for the latent rate whose **half-width is a monotone increasing function of said upper confidence bound** and additively of a bounded common-mode parameter;
- an **actuation controller** configured to compare, per spatial unit, said upper confidence bound and said half-width against thresholds and, upon exceedance, to (i) flag the unit, (ii) automatically trigger acquisition of an additional measurement channel or invoke a small-area-estimation subroutine for that unit, and (iii) gate a downstream automated resource-scheduling output pending re-evaluation.

*(The three specific channel types, the per-unit gating, and the trigger-to-reacquire loop are the anti-*Electric Power Group* limitations: this is not "analyze and display," it is a self-diagnosing controller that changes its own computation and I/O based on an internal consistency test.)*

**Claim 2 (dependent, the gating specifics — the real inventive core).**
The system of claim 1, wherein the conformal half-width comprises a first term $f(\hat\delta^{+}_\perp)$ that is **estimated from and increasing in** the over-identification upper confidence bound and a second, additive term $g(\Gamma)$ that is invariant to the channel data, and wherein the actuation controller distinguishes units in which the first term dominates (triggering channel reacquisition) from units in which the second term dominates (triggering a human-review flag rather than reacquisition).

*(This is where the genuine non-obviousness lives: splitting the robustness radius into a data-driven, test-gated part and a data-blind part, and routing on which dominates. Nothing in the cited art does this.)*

**Claim 3 (independent, method).**
A computer-implemented method of calibrated latent-rate monitoring, comprising, in order:
1. receiving signals from at least three institutionally mechanism-independent channels for a plurality of spatial units;
2. deconvolving a pivot-channel residual law from the joint characteristic function;
3. computing, per unit, a pairwise-reconstruction discrepancy statistic and a one-sided bound thereon;
4. forming, per unit, a conformal prediction set whose width is increased as a monotone function of said bound;
5. **automatically actuating** a machine response conditioned on said bound crossing a threshold, the response comprising initiating acquisition of a further channel and withholding a downstream automated schedule for the affected unit until step 4 is repeated with the further channel.

*Steps 5's "initiate acquisition + withhold + recompute" is the physical/technical closed loop that lifts the method out of pure mental-process territory (cf. McRO/Diehr).*

---

## 4. Closest prior art and the non-obvious delta

| Reference | What it discloses | Delta OICC must own |
|---|---|---|
| **Jin–Ren–Candès, conformal sensitivity (PNAS 2023)** | Conformal set inflated by a **user-specified** confounding radius Γ | OICC makes the radius **data-driven from an over-ID statistic** and **splits** it into gated + data-blind parts. *This is the strongest §103 distinction.* |
| **Vovk et al. conformal prediction (foundational)** | Distribution-free prediction sets | OICC targets a **latent, never-labeled** quantity via a deconvolved residual score — not a conventional CP use |
| **Angelopoulos/PPI, Csillag et al. (2023–25)** | Correct an estimand using observed/gold labels | OICC has **no label**; the "test-gated width" loop is absent |
| **Kotlarski 1967 / Hu–Schennach 2008** | Nonparametric deconvolution (pure math) | Not patentable; used as an admitted building block |
| **Hansen 1982 / Sargan over-ID (GMM)** | Over-identification specification test | Never wired to **prediction-set width or actuation** |
| **PredPol / HunchLab / Palantir predictive-policing patents (e.g., US 8,949,164 and family)** | Forecast crime → allocate resources | **FTO risk, not just novelty**: these dominate the "forecast→dispatch" actuation. OICC must distinguish on the *self-diagnosing calibration loop*, and should avoid claiming the dispatch itself |
| **Kalman/sensor-fusion patents** | Multi-sensor state estimation with residual innovation gating | *Closest structural analog*: innovation-gated fusion exists. OICC's delta is the **conformal, distribution-free, partial-identification** treatment and the **detectable/undetectable split** — argue this is non-obvious over innovation gating, but expect the examiner to cite fusion art |

**The single defensible non-obvious delta, in one sentence:** *a conformal monitoring controller whose prediction-set width and actuation are gated, per spatial unit, by a cross-channel over-identification consistency statistic, with the robustness radius partitioned into a test-estimated component and a provably-data-blind component that route to different machine responses.* If any one of those elements is dropped, the claim collapses into Jin–Ren–Candès + Electric Power Group and dies.

---

## 5. Risks you must weigh before filing

1. **§101 is only weakly cleared.** An examiner citing *Electric Power Group* is a coin-flip at best; you win only if the reacquire/withhold/recompute loop is treated as a technical improvement to the system's own operation (Enfish/McRO). Prosecution will be a fight.
2. **§103 pressure from Kalman innovation-gating.** "Use a residual/consistency statistic to gate downstream action" is a known control-systems motif. Your non-obviousness rests narrowly on the *conformal + partial-ID + detectable/undetectable split*. Defensible but not comfortable.
3. **FTO / blocking art in predictive policing.** The PredPol family and Palantir portfolios cover forecast-to-allocation. Claiming the *dispatch* actuation invites both a §103 rejection and an infringement exposure. **Recommendation: claim the calibration/self-diagnosis loop and a generic "gate a downstream automated schedule," never the policing decision.**
4. **Enablement is fine; the unproven proofs don't matter for patenting.** A patent needs enablement, not correctness of a theorem. The pipeline is codeable (the §"codeable form" block), so §112 enablement is satisfiable even though the load-bearing nonclassical proof (obligation (i)) is incomplete for publication. This is a rare case where the patent is *easier* to support than the paper.
5. **Value/enforcement is low.** The realistic practitioners are academics and government agencies; damages and licensing markets are thin. Even a granted patent is easy to design around (change the gating function, use 2 channels + external restriction, use online conformal). The commercial ROI is questionable.

---

## 6. Strategic recommendation

- **Do not patent the theorem or the estimator math.** Confirmed unpatentable; publish it for the academic credit instead (defensive publication also blocks others).
- **File one narrow provisional** on the **closed-loop system of Claims 1–2** — specifically the *over-ID-statistic-gated conformal width + detectable/undetectable split + reacquire/withhold actuation* — **decoupled from crime**. Draft the spec with epidemiology, network-anomaly, and industrial-sensor-fusion embodiments so the claims read as a general **self-calibrating multi-channel monitoring controller**; crime is one embodiment. This maximizes both eligibility (technical framing) and FTO distance from PredPol.
- **Claim the actuation as machine self-modification** (reacquire channel / invoke small-area subroutine / withhold automated schedule), **never as a human dispatch decision.**
- **Best realistic outcome:** a narrow granted system patent of modest enforcement value. That is why the honest number is **3.5/10** — eligible-if-fought, arguably novel on the coupling, but obviousness-pressured, FTO-encumbered, and low-value. The dominant asset here remains the *paper*, not the patent.

--- INTEGRATION 3 ---
\section{Methods}
\label{sec:methods}

\subsection{Problem setup and notation}

We forecast a latent victimization intensity that is never directly observed. Let $a\in\{1,\dots,A\}$ index spatial units and $t\in\{1,\dots,T\}$ index periods, and write the observational unit as $i=(a,t)$. The target of inference is the latent log-rate
\[
\theta_{i}\;=\;\log\lambda(a,t)\in\mathbb{R},
\]
where $\lambda(a,t)$ is the true rate at which victimizing events occur in unit $i$, as opposed to the rate at which they are \emph{recorded} by any one institution. All administrative crime series measure $\lambda$ only through an institutional filter whose selectivity is itself a function of $\lambda$ and of area disparity, which is the source of the observation-biased feedback our program targets. We therefore treat every observed series as a noisy channel of a common latent factor and identify the distribution of $\theta$ by repeated-measurement deconvolution, then wrap a conformal predictor around the deconvolved target.

Throughout, $\varphi_{Z}(t)=\mathbb{E}[e^{\mathrm{i}tZ}]$ denotes a characteristic function (CF), $*$ convolution, $\mathrm{TV}(\cdot,\cdot)$ total variation, and $\|\cdot\|_{\infty}$ the sup-norm of a CDF. Calibration and test index sets are $\mathcal I_{\mathrm{cal}}$ and the forecast target $n{+}1$.

\subsection{The multi-channel measurement model}

We observe $K\ge 3$ \emph{mechanism-independent} channels of the same latent process. Channels are selected by generating mechanism, not data source: series that pass through a single institutional filter (police records, arrests, cleared cases) collapse to \emph{one} channel, and the independent set must cross institutions. On the log scale we posit the one-factor measurement model
\begin{equation}
Y^{c}_{i}\;=\;\alpha_{c}+\beta_{c}\,\theta_{i}+\varepsilon^{c}_{i},
\qquad c=1,\dots,K,
\label{eq:factor}
\end{equation}
with the standard normalization $\alpha_{1}=0,\ \beta_{1}=1$, so that channel~1 is an observable pivot, $Y^{1}_{i}=\theta_{i}+\varepsilon^{1}_{i}$. In the U.S.\ spine the channels are police records, the National Crime Victimization Survey (NCVS) as a respondent-recall channel, and 911 calls-for-service as a public-initiation channel; in the India spine they are police IPC records, the state--year distress-call series (\texttt{PC3\_Distress\_call\_over\_phone\_No\_100\_etc}), and the institutional-violence channels (complaints against police, human-rights violations by police, custodial deaths), whose recording error is \emph{anti-correlated} with police-record error and thus supplies the strongest available independence structure.

Crucially, police under-recording is \emph{nonclassical}: the error $\varepsilon^{c}$ correlates with the true $\theta$ because high-rate, high-disparity areas are recorded least completely. The classical Kotlarski assumption of $\theta$-independent additive error \citep{kotlarski1967} is therefore inadequate as the backbone, and we route identification through the nonclassical measurement-error framework of \citet{huschennach2008}.

\subsection{Identification and specification testing}

\paragraph{Identification.}
Under conditional independence of the channel errors given $(\theta,X)$ and CF/completeness regularity (Assumptions A1--A3, \S\ref{sec:assumptions}), the latent law $f_{\theta}$ and each channel error law $f_{\varepsilon^{c}}$ are nonparametrically identified. With two \emph{classical} channels, Kotlarski's identity point-identifies $f_\theta$ from the joint CF \citep{kotlarski1967,livuong1998}:
\begin{equation}
\varphi_{\theta}(t)\;=\;\exp\!\Big(\textstyle\int_{0}^{t}
\frac{\partial_{2}\varphi_{Y^{1},Y^{2}}(s,0)}{\varphi_{Y^{1},Y^{2}}(s,0)}\,ds\Big).
\label{eq:kotlarski}
\end{equation}
Because crime recording is nonclassical, we instead use the operator-diagonalization identity of \citet{huschennach2008}, in which the observed conditional-density operators satisfy $L_{W,y\mid Z}\,(L_{W\mid Z})^{-1}=L_{W\mid\theta}\,D_{y\mid\theta}\,L_{W\mid\theta}^{-1}$, so that the eigen-decomposition recovers $f_{\theta}$ and the nonclassical kernels under a median-zero (rather than mean-zero) normalization. With $K\ge 3$ channels the loadings $\beta_{c}$ are freed and identified from higher-order cumulants \citep{bonhommerobin2010,cunhaheckmanschennach2010}.

\paragraph{The just-identification correction.}
A factor-analysis referee will observe that with \emph{exactly} $K=3$ indicators the one-factor model is just-identified in second moments: $\mathrm{Cov}(Y^{j},Y^{k})=\beta_{j}\beta_{k}\sigma_{\theta}^{2}$ gives three equations in three unknowns and zero degrees of freedom. We state explicitly that with $K=3$ the over-identifying content lives \emph{entirely in the higher-order cumulants and the full joint CF}, not in the covariance matrix; covariance tetrad tests require $K\ge 4$.

\paragraph{Specification test.}
The over-identifying restrictions furnish a specification test the single-anchor program lacked. Let $\{\hat f_{\theta}^{(jk)}\}_{j<k}$ be the pairwise deconvolution recoveries, which coincide under the maintained model. Their discrepancy defines the over-identification statistic
\begin{equation}
\hat\delta_{\perp}
=\Big(\textstyle\sum_{j<k}\big\|\hat f_{\theta}^{(jk)}-\bar f_{\theta}\big\|_{L^{2}}^{2}\Big)^{1/2}
\;\xrightarrow{P}\;\psi\big(\|\Delta_{\perp}\|\big),
\label{eq:overid}
\end{equation}
equivalently a Hansen--Sargan/CF $J$-statistic, with $\psi$ strictly increasing. We are deliberate about what rejection means: it falsifies the \emph{conjunction} $\{$one-factor $\wedge$ conditional independence $\wedge$ normalization$\}$ in identifiable directions; it does not verify conditional independence, and non-rejection carries the usual finite-power caveat \citep{shahpeters2020}. The test has provably \emph{zero} power against the factor-aligned common-mode direction $\Delta_{\parallel}$ (\S\ref{sec:assumptions}), which we import as a matching impossibility rather than conceal.

\subsection{Conformal prediction of the deconvolved latent}

Because $\theta_{i}$ is observed on no unit, the ideal nonconformity score $|\theta_{i}-\hat\theta_{i}|$ is not computable, and conformalizing any channel $Y^{c}$ yields valid coverage of a \emph{biased proxy}, not of $\theta$. Distribution-free finite-sample coverage of $\theta$ is in fact impossible without a label (a formal statement and proof appear in the companion theorem section): two admissible laws differing only in $\Delta_{\parallel}$ share every observable marginal while shifting $\theta$ arbitrarily. Our predictor is the constructive complement that covers the deconvolved estimand exactly up to estimable error.

\paragraph{Latent-scale score via a leave-the-pivot-out residual.}
Let the plug-in latent predictor use only the non-pivot channels,
\begin{equation}
\hat\theta_{i}=m\big(Y^{2}_{i},\dots,Y^{K}_{i}\big)
=\mathbb{E}_{\hat f_{\theta},\,\hat f_{\varepsilon^{2:K}}}\!\big[\theta\mid Y^{2:K}\big],
\end{equation}
the empirical-Bayes deconvolution posterior mean. Define the infeasible latent residual and the feasible pivot residual
\begin{equation}
S_{i}=\theta_{i}-\hat\theta_{i}\ (\text{unobserved}),
\qquad
R_{i}=Y^{1}_{i}-\hat\theta_{i}=S_{i}+\varepsilon^{1}_{i}\ (\text{observed}).
\end{equation}
Since $\hat\theta_{i}$ uses only channels $\{2,\dots,K\}$, conditional independence makes $\varepsilon^{1}$ independent of $S$, so $F_{R}=F_{S}*F_{\varepsilon^{1}}$, i.e.\ $\varphi_{S}(t)=\varphi_{R}(t)/\varphi_{\varepsilon^{1}}(t)$. The computable score $R_{i}$ thus has a law equal to the latent-residual law convolved with the identified pivot error; deconvolving by $f_{\varepsilon^{1}}$ recovers the score law we cannot observe. This is the mechanism by which a latent-target conformal set is constructed, and it differs fundamentally from PPI and conformal-powered PPI \citep{angelopoulos2023ppi,csillag2025}, which observe the target on a gold-standard sample, and from conformal sensitivity for counterfactuals \citep{jin2023sensitivity}, which perturbs an unmeasured confounder rather than deconvolving a latent construct.

\paragraph{Sensitivity coupling.}
Split-conformal quantiles $\hat q_{\alpha/2},\hat q_{1-\alpha/2}$ of the regularized deconvolution $\hat F_{S}$ \citep{romano2019cqr} define a base set $[\hat\theta_{i}+\hat q_{\alpha/2},\ \hat\theta_{i}+\hat q_{1-\alpha/2}]$. We then couple the specification slack to the interval width. Writing the off-diagonal error covariance as $\Delta=\Delta_{\perp}\oplus\Delta_{\parallel}$ with detectable radius $\|\Delta_{\perp}\|\le\tau$ and factor-aligned radius $\|\Delta_{\parallel}\|\le\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$, the independence-assuming deconvolution mis-estimates the residual variance by $-2\kappa$, $\kappa=w^{\top}\rho$, $|\kappa|\le\|w\|\tau$, where $w$ are the plug-in predictor weights. Restoring worst-case coverage gives the data-driven inflation
\begin{equation}
f(\tau)=z_{1-\alpha/2}\Big(\sqrt{\hat v_{S}^{\mathrm{naive}}+2\|w\|\tau}-\sqrt{\hat v_{S}^{\mathrm{naive}}}\Big),
\qquad
g(\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}})=z_{1-\alpha/2}\Big(\sqrt{\hat v_{S}^{\mathrm{naive}}+\|w\|^{2}\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}}-\sqrt{\hat v_{S}^{\mathrm{naive}}}\Big),
\label{eq:inflation}
\end{equation}
with $\hat v_{S}^{\mathrm{naive}}=\mathrm{Var}(R)-\hat\sigma_{1}^{2}$. The argument of $f$ is the estimated $\hat\delta_{\perp}$, so the set widens exactly where the over-identification tripwire fires; $g$ is the sole user knob, acting only on the data-blind $\Delta_{\parallel}$ direction. The calibrated set is
\begin{equation}
\widehat C^{\theta,\Gamma}_{\alpha}(i)=\hat\theta_{i}+\big[\hat q_{\alpha/2}-f-g,\ \hat q_{1-\alpha/2}+f+g\big].
\end{equation}
Under a true perturbation in the ball, coverage satisfies the master bound
\begin{equation}
\mathbb{P}\big(\theta_{n+1}\in\widehat C^{\theta,\Gamma}_{\alpha}\big)
\ge 1-\alpha-2\xi_{n}-2\,\mathrm{TV}(\hat w,w)-2\beta_{\mathrm{mix}}(\ell)-\beta-\tfrac{1}{n_{\mathrm{blk}}+1},
\label{eq:master}
\end{equation}
where $\xi_{n}=\|\hat F_{S}-F_{S}\|_{\infty}$ is the estimable deconvolution error (ordinary-smooth rate $n^{-\kappa/(2\kappa+2p+1)}$; supersmooth/Gaussian-recall rate $(\log n)^{-\kappa/r}$), $\mathrm{TV}(\hat w,w)$ the covariate/time-shift weight error under weighted conformal \citep{tibshirani2019}, $\beta_{\mathrm{mix}}(\ell)$ the $\beta$-mixing coefficient at block-gap $\ell$, and $n_{\mathrm{blk}}$ the number of near-independent blocks. The unidentifiable $\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$ is paid in \emph{width} through $g$, never as a claimed-coverage debit.

\subsection{Estimator algorithm}

\begin{enumerate}
\item \textbf{Channel assembly.} Group series by generating mechanism into $K\ge 3$ mechanism-independent channels at the common latent resolution (metro--month for the U.S.\ spine; state--year for India).
\item \textbf{Deconvolution.} Estimate $f_{\theta}$ and $\{f_{\varepsilon^{c}}\}$ by nonclassical Hu--Schennach operator diagonalization (classical Kotlarski as a special case), regularizing the inverse-CF by a Tikhonov ridge $\varrho_{n}$ and CF-kernel $K_{h}$.
\item \textbf{Specification test.} Compute pairwise recoveries $\hat f_{\theta}^{(jk)}$ and the over-identification statistic $\hat\delta_{\perp}$ \eqref{eq:overid}; obtain its one-sided $(1-\beta)$ upper confidence bound $\hat\delta_{\perp}^{+}$ by bootstrap. Report $\hat\delta_{\perp}$ per area as a tripwire.
\item \textbf{Scores.} Form $\hat\theta_{i}=m(Y^{2:K}_{i})$ and pivot residuals $R_{i}=Y^{1}_{i}-\hat\theta_{i}$ on $\mathcal I_{\mathrm{cal}}$.
\item \textbf{Latent-residual law.} Deconvolve the empirical CF of $\{R_{i}\}$ by $\hat f_{\varepsilon^{1}}$ to obtain $\hat F_{S}$ and quantiles $\hat q_{\alpha/2},\hat q_{1-\alpha/2}$.
\item \textbf{Panel repair.} Reweight the calibration CDF by the covariate/time-shift likelihood ratio $\hat w$ and thin into $\ell$-separated blocks (by area for the India field).
\item \textbf{Sensitivity inflation.} Evaluate $f(\hat\delta_{\perp}^{+})$ and $g(\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}})$ from \eqref{eq:inflation}.
\item \textbf{Set.} Return $\widehat C^{\theta,\Gamma}_{\alpha}(i)$ and verify coverage against \eqref{eq:master}.
\end{enumerate}

\subsection{Assumptions, limitations, and estimand resolution}
\label{sec:assumptions}

\paragraph{What is assumed.}
(A1) Conditional independence of channel errors given $(\theta,X)$; (A2) Kotlarski/Hu--Schennach CF and bounded-completeness regularity; (A3) scale ($\beta_{1}=1$) and location (median-zero, nonclassical) normalization; (A4) calibration exchangeability, relaxed to weighted/blocked exchangeability on panels; (A5) a bounded common-mode budget $\|\Delta_{\parallel}\|\le\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$.

\paragraph{What is testable, and what is not.}
The over-identifying restrictions make the detectable subspace $\Delta_{\perp}$ falsifiable: rejection falsifies the maintained conjunction in identifiable directions, and $\hat\delta_{\perp}$ consistently estimates a monotone functional of $\|\Delta_{\perp}\|$. We explicitly do \emph{not} claim to make conditional independence testable. The factor-aligned common-mode component $\Delta_{\parallel}$—systematic under-recording co-loading on the latent in the same direction across channels—is absorbed into the estimated factor and is unidentifiable at every $K$; no estimator or test can bound it from data. It is worst precisely in the sparse, high-disparity cells where the over-identification test is least powered, the continuous analogue of the latent-class non-identifiability degeneracy in multiple-systems estimation \citep{manriquevallier2016}. This is the prior fatal objection surviving in new clothing: OICC does not dissolve it but quarantines it into the single provably-minimal knob $\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$, with coverage degrading visibly in that knob rather than silently.

\paragraph{Estimand resolution.}
Channels must measure the same latent unit. In India every mechanism-independent channel is published at state--year resolution, so the estimand is a state--year rate field ($\sim$34--36 areas $\times$ 14 years $\approx$ 480 cells) and the district IPC series enters only as a downscaling covariate; the small block count makes the atom $1/(n_{\mathrm{blk}}+1)$ non-negligible and it is reported, not hidden. The U.S.\ estimand is metro--month, with the honest caveat that NCVS is not published at fine sub-national geography and its area-level channel requires small-area estimation—a real cost. Cross-nationally, U.S.\ under-recording is victim-side (non-reporting) and India's is police-side (burking/FIR refusal); a method whose tripwire passes in low-disparity and fires in high-disparity cells in both countries, tracking each country's distinct known bias direction, is identifying a mechanism-invariant signal that a country-specific confound cannot game.

\bibliographystyle{plainnat}

Word count is approximately 1,950. The section is self-contained and LaTeX-ready; it assumes `amsmath`, `amssymb`, and `natbib` in the preamble, and the following `\bibitem` keys must be supplied in the bibliography: `kotlarski1967`, `livuong1998`, `huschennach2008`, `bonhommerobin2010`, `cunhaheckmanschennach2010`, `shahpeters2020`, `jin2023sensitivity`, `angelopoulos2023ppi`, `csillag2025`, `romano2019cqr`, `tibshirani2019`, `manriquevallier2016`.

Three deliberate reviewer-proofing choices worth flagging to the calling author: (1) the specification test is stated to falsify only the conjunction {one-factor ∧ CI ∧ normalization}, never "CI is testable," matching the honesty constraint; (2) the $K=3$ just-identification correction is stated inline so a factor-analysis referee cannot sink it; (3) the $\Delta_\parallel$ impossibility and $\Gamma_{\mathrm{cm}}$ minimality are surfaced in the Limitations subsection rather than buried, and the estimand resolution ceiling (India state–year, US metro–month with NCVS small-area caveat) is declared explicitly. The impossibility proposition and full coverage theorem are referenced as living in the companion theorem section, keeping this Methods section at the target length.

---

# PART IV — HOSTILE RED-TEAM (the reviewer you least want)

I now have what I need. Let me write the hostile reviewer's verdict.

---

# REVIEWER REPORT — Recommendation: **REJECT** (borderline desk-reject; salvageable only as a heavily-qualified applied-stats paper)

I am the reviewer you least wanted: I have published in nonclassical measurement-error identification *and* in conformal inference. I read the theorem section, the integration spec, and the honesty subsections. The program is far more careful than the median submission — the authors have pre-conceded most of the obvious kills. That itself is the tell. **A method whose own limitations section contains the fatal objection, restated three times in escalating precision, is a method that has already lost the argument and is negotiating the sentence.** Let me make the four attacks you asked for, then rank.

---

## Attack 1 — Does the over-ID test make anchor-independence testable? **No. It tests a strictly weaker implication, and the fatal direction is provably invisible at every K.**

This is the load-bearing claim of the whole "ceiling-break," and it is the one the authors have already surrendered in §4/§6/§8 — I am merely going to deny them the credit they try to salvage from the surrender.

The clean statement of what is happening: the one-factor model with K=3 imposes exactly the vanishing-tetrad / higher-cumulant restrictions on the *joint observable law*. Those restrictions are a **necessary consequence** of {one-factor ∧ CI ∧ normalization}, not equivalent to CI. The authors admit this. But here is the part that is fatal rather than merely limiting, and it is confirmed by the deconfounder literature I pulled: **a shared confounder that co-loads on the channels in the factor direction is absorbed into the estimated factor and leaves the entire observable law — all moments, all cumulants, all K — invariant.** D'Amour's non-identification result and Wang–Blei's own discussants establish that even a *uniquely identified* factor model does not identify the causal/latent object of interest under shared confounding; under linear-Gaussian treatments the target "is not identifiable without parametric assumptions" ([D'Amour 2018](https://www.alexdamour.com/blog/public/2018/05/18/non-identification-in-latent-confounder-models/); [discussion of Wang–Blei](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1910.06991)).

So the honest reduction is:

- Prior round: anchor-independence **untestable in principle**.
- This round: {factor ∧ CI ∧ norm} **falsifiable in the Δ⊥ subspace, provably powerless in the Δ∥ subspace**.

The authors sell this as "+1 to +1.5 novelty." I price it lower, for a reason they do not confront: **the Δ∥ direction is not a measure-zero pathology — it is the physically expected data-generating direction for this exact application.** Under-recording that rises with true disorder in high-disparity areas loads *the same way* on records, on calls-that-get-logged, and on complaints-that-get-filed, because all three pass through institutions responding to the same disorder. The unidentifiable direction is not the adversarial corner case; it is the modal hypothesis about how bias works. A sensitivity analysis that is blind in precisely the direction the domain expects the bias to point is not a partial solution — it is a null result dressed as a robustness radius. **The over-ID test converts an untestable assumption into a testable-but-irrelevant one.** That is a real but small methodological increment, not a ceiling-break.

Verdict on Attack 1: **the strong claim is false; the authors know it is false; the surviving weak claim is worth ~+0.5, not +1.5.**

---

## Attack 2 — Is deconvolution + conformal scooped? **Not exactly, but the combination is thinner than claimed, and the nearest neighbors compress the novelty.**

I searched. Honest findings:

- **No paper wraps conformal prediction around a *repeated-measurement-deconvolved latent* target.** The closest measurement-error conformal paper ([Singer, Williams & Ghosh, MNRAS 2025](https://arxiv.org/html/2412.10544)) explicitly covers the **observed** error-contaminated response, not a latent one, and uses elliptical-residual scaling, not CF deconvolution. It does not scoop the target object. Good.
- **BUT** the "novel object" is a *composition* of four fully-scooped primitives (Kotlarski/Hu–Schennach; Hansen–Sargan over-ID; Jin–Ren–Candès sensitivity radius; split conformal), and the composition's load-bearing move — "make the sensitivity radius data-driven from a specification statistic instead of a user knob" — is exactly the increment a JRC-literate reviewer will call **the obvious next step**. Data-driving a sensitivity parameter from an estimable moment discrepancy is not a new *idea*; innovation-gated fusion has done residual→action for decades (the patent memo concedes this against Kalman gating). The novelty is real but it is *arrangement* novelty, and arrangement novelty is a KDD-ADS/AOAS contribution, not a NeurIPS-main theorem.
- The one genuinely new formal object — **finite-sample-flavored conformal coverage of a deconvolved target with the radius split into estimated (Δ⊥) and provably-minimal (Δ∥) parts** — is not in the literature. I grant that. But its content is dominated by an *impossibility* (Prop. 4.1) and a *first-order/Gaussian-only* constructive result. The constructive theorem that would justify the top rating (the exact nonclassical Hu–Schennach decomposition with "power ∝ ‖Δ⊥‖, zero on Δ∥") is **stated as a proof obligation, not proved**. The authors flag this themselves. I cannot rate an unproven theorem as a theorem.

Verdict on Attack 2: **not scooped, but the unclaimed part is (i) an impossibility result and (ii) a Gaussian first-order calculation, with the crown-jewel nonclassical theorem still open. That is a promissory note, not a contribution.**

---

## Attack 3 — Do the real crime channels satisfy conditional independence given θ? **No. The empirical literature says the shared causes are first-order, not residual — and they load in the unfalsifiable direction.**

This is where the paper actually dies as a *causal* identification claim, independent of the theory. The whole edifice rests on A1: εᶜ mutually independent given (θ, X). The crime-measurement literature I pulled says this is false in exactly the way that matters:

- **Media attention** raises reporting propensity *and* public concern *and* enforcement attention "regardless of actual crime statistics" ([Reddit-discourse crime study](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2512.18227); [calls-for-service measurement error, NIJ](https://nij.ojp.gov/library/publications/investigating-scope-measurement-error-calls-service-measure-crime)). That is a common cause of records **and** calls **and** complaints not routed through θ.
- **Policing intensity** inflates recorded crime and shapes which calls get logged as crimes — the [Journal of Experimental Criminology bias study](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11292-021-09457-y) shows selection biases worsen at fine geography, exactly the regime the method targets.
- Officers encounter ~30% more crime than is called in, and CFS↔records discrepancies vary by crime type ([NIJ CFS study](https://nij.ojp.gov/library/publications/investigating-scope-measurement-error-calls-service-measure-crime)) — i.e., the errors share type-specific and enforcement-specific structure.

Now combine this with Attack 1. These shared causes (media, policing intensity, disorder) are not orthogonal noise you can push into Δ⊥ and detect. They **co-load the police-sourced channels in the same direction** — the Δ∥ subspace — because they are the mechanism by which institutional filtering tracks disorder. So the empirically-documented CI violations are *precisely* the ones the over-ID test cannot see. The India "adversarial institution / anti-correlated error" argument is the one place this is partially mitigated — and the integration spec itself concedes C2 (distress calls) is **logged by police**, collapsing it toward C1's filter and threatening K=3→K=2 (point-ID, **no over-ID test at all**). The single most independent channel is the weakest link, and the authors say so.

Verdict on Attack 3: **conditional independence is violated by documented, first-order common causes that load in the invisible direction. This is not a modeling nicety; it is the identification assumption failing where the paper needs it most.** Fatal to the strong causal claim.

---

## Attack 4 — Is the cross-national replication real evidence or theater? **Mostly theater as *identification*; real but modest as *external-validity corroboration* — and the authors have already retreated to the defensible version.**

The E2 "mechanism-invariance" test is genuinely the best idea in the program: US bias is victim-side, India's is police-side, so a country-specific confound can't explain a gap that tracks each country's *different* known disparity driver. I like it. But:

- India's identification panel is **≤350 state-year cells, K=3 with zero redundancy, over-ID content only in noisy third cumulants, 2001–2010 window** (per the integration spec's own verified counts). Third-cumulant over-ID on N≤350 has, to be blunt, **no power**. The India "over-ID tripwire" is not going to fire reliably on anything; a null there is uninformative, not confirmatory.
- The latent construct differs (IPC aggregate vs UCR index) — the authors correctly forbid level comparison and restrict to the gap-vs-disparity *relationship*. That restriction is honest but it also means the cross-national test reduces to: "in two countries, our estimated under-recording gap correlates with a locally-plausible disparity proxy." That is a **correlational corroboration of a pre-registered sign**, not evidence that the deconvolution identified θ. A spurious common driver (both proxies correlate with development/urbanization) can produce the same pattern.
- E4 ("Γcm minimality demonstration") is **circular as evidence**: perturbing the data by a rank-one β βᵀ shift and confirming the cumulants don't move is not an empirical finding — it is a numerical re-derivation of Prop. 4.1. Presenting an algebraic identity as an experiment is the kind of thing that gets a paper desk-rejected by a referee who notices.

Verdict on Attack 4: **the cross-national arm is legitimate as a falsification *ritual* (pre-registered sign, publicly failable) but weak as *evidence*, and one of its four experiments is a tautology. It corroborates external validity; it does not identify anything. The authors' own §6 already concedes "India does not lift novelty."**

---

## Ranked objections

**FATAL (each independently caps the paper below the target):**
1. **The unidentifiable Δ∥ direction is the empirically-modal bias direction, not a corner case** (Attacks 1+3). The over-ID test is blind exactly where crime bias is documented to live. This is the prior fatal objection, alive, in new clothing — as the authors admit in §8.
2. **The crown-jewel nonclassical theorem is unproven** (Attack 2). What exists is an impossibility result plus a Gaussian-first-order calculation. NeurIPS-main needs the theorem, not the promissory note.
3. **Conditional independence is contradicted by first-order empirical common causes** (Attack 3), and the most-independent channel (distress calls) is police-logged, risking collapse to K=2 with no test at all.

**SERIOUS (survivable with heavy qualification):**
4. Composition/arrangement novelty over Jin–Ren–Candès + fusion gating is incremental; a JRC-literate reviewer calls data-driving the radius "the obvious next step."
5. India panel (≤350 cells, K=3, third-cumulant over-ID) has no finite-sample power; the cross-national "test" cannot fail informatively.
6. E4 is a tautology presented as an experiment.

**SURVIVING (genuine, creditable):**
7. The leave-pivot-out residual R = S + ε¹ as a *computable* score whose *law* deconvolves to the latent-residual law is clever and, as far as I can find, new. This is the real contribution.
8. Prop. 4.1 (impossibility of distribution-free finite-sample latent coverage) is correct, clean, and honestly foregrounded.
9. The visible-degradation property (width grows where δ̂⊥ fires) is a genuinely nice honesty feature even if δ̂⊥ is underpowered.
10. Cross-national pre-registration of a *sign that can fail publicly* is good scientific hygiene.

---

## Honest maximum this can reach

Let me separate the two things the authors keep conflating.

**As a NeurIPS-main / D&B contribution: it does not clear the bar. Ceiling stays where the prior round put it.** The reason is not that the work is bad — it is that the one new *theorem* is an impossibility plus a Gaussian sketch, and the one new *idea* (data-driven sensitivity radius) is arrangement-novelty over JRC that is powerless in the domain's modal bias direction. Neither is a new learning-theoretic result. The program is honest enough to have written its own rejection into §8.

- **Novelty: 6.5** (not the claimed 7.5–8.0). The leave-pivot-out score and the Δ⊥/Δ∥ split are real and earn the +0 over prior only because the fatal direction remains invisible and the flagship theorem is unproven. Prove the exact nonclassical decomposition and I move to 7.0.
- **Publication (venue-adjusted): 7.5.** This is a strong **KDD-ADS / FAccT / AOAS / Journal of Quantitative Criminology** paper *if and only if* the title and abstract claim "**falsifiable-in-identifiable-directions with a provably-minimal residual**" and **never** "makes conditional independence testable." At those venues, an honest partial-identification + conformal + real cross-national data paper with a self-aware limitations section is publishable and useful.
- **Target: unchanged from prior round.** KDD-ADS/FAccT. Not NeurIPS-main.

**The single sentence that decides the paper's fate:** if the abstract says the multi-channel deconvolution *makes the killer assumption testable* or *dissolves the objection*, I desk-reject on Attack 1 alone. If it says the program *quarantines an untestable assumption into a provably-minimal knob whose blind direction coincides with the domain's expected bias, and reports coverage that degrades visibly in that knob* — then it is an honest, mid-strong applied-statistics paper and I recommend acceptance at an applied venue with major revisions (prove the nonclassical decomposition; drop E4; downgrade India to corroboration; foreground the police-logging threat to C2).

**Bottom line:** The ceiling-break hypothesis **fails**. Three or more channels do not dissolve the fatal objection — they relocate it from "untestable in principle" to "testable but powerless in the one direction that matters," which for this application is a distinction without a practical difference. The program is a genuine, honest **+0.5** improvement in rigor and a clean applied-stats paper. It is not a NeurIPS-main paper, and its own limitations section is the most persuasive evidence for that verdict.

Sources: [D'Amour, non-identification in latent confounder models](https://www.alexdamour.com/blog/public/2018/05/18/non-identification-in-latent-confounder-models/) · [Discussion of "Blessings of Multiple Causes"](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1910.06991) · [Singer, Williams & Ghosh, Conformal Prediction for Astronomy Data with Measurement Error (MNRAS 2025)](https://arxiv.org/html/2412.10544) · [Delaigle–Hall–Meister, deconvolution with repeated measurements](https://arxiv.org/pdf/0804.0713) · [Police data bias & geographic crime analysis (J Exp Criminol)](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11292-021-09457-y) · [NIJ, measurement error in calls-for-service](https://nij.ojp.gov/library/publications/investigating-scope-measurement-error-calls-service-measure-crime) · [Fortified proximal causal inference with many imperfect negative controls](https://www.vicbiostat.org.au/event/fortified-proximal-causal-inference-many-imperfect-negative-controls)

# PART V — FINAL MASTER PLAN v2 + HONEST VERDICT

Data confirmed: distress-call column is `PC3_Distress_call_over_phoneNo_100_etc`; all three independent India channels present. Writing the definitive plan.
